NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN FRANCISCO, Wednesday, May 06 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, ProLogis has shown some strength in recent days. ProLogis (NYSE:PLD), lifted 1.9% during its previous trading session to close at $142.90.

ProLogis, Inc. (NYSE:PLD) is a leading global provider of logistics real estate solutions, strategically enhancing supply chain efficiency through innovative industrial properties. With an extensive portfolio including logistics parks like Prologis Park Sumner and North Chennai logistics park, the company demonstrates strong market presence in industrial real estate. It is actively developing sustainable logistics solutions and expanding its renewable energy portfolio in collaboration with partners such as the SolarCycle partnership. Prologis's competitive strengths lie in its scale and strategic joint ventures, such as the €1B seed assets JV. Its adaptation to regional needs, evidenced by initiatives like Prologis India development projects, underscores its commitment to long-term growth.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Prologis’ Jeff Bray Honored as 2026 Risk Manager of the Year

RIMS has named Jeff Bray, senior vice president and head of global risk management at Prologis, as the 2026 Risk Manager of the Year. The announcement comes as RIMS prepares for the RISKWORLD conference in Philadelphia next month, where Bray will receive his award. The date of the announcement, 27th April 2026, highlights the ongoing commitment of RIMS to recognize leaders in the risk management profession.

The Risk Manager of the Year program, which began in 1977, is designed to elevate the profile of the risk management profession by spotlighting exceptional contributions made by individuals within their respective organizations. Emily Buckley, insurance risk manager for Specialized Bicycle Components, has also been honored with induction into the Risk Management Honor Roll. RIMS CEO Gary LaBranche remarked, "The best leaders in this field don't just protect value, they help create it," underscoring the impact of Bray's innovative strategies at Prologis.

Prologis, headquartered in San Francisco, is a leading player in logistics real estate, managing approximately 1.3 billion square feet across 20 countries. Since his tenure began at AMB Property Corporation in 2005 and subsequent leadership role post-AMB's merger with Prologis in 2011, Bray has developed a comprehensive global risk management framework. His approach integrates insurance programs, claims management, and strategies for addressing emerging risks, thus aligning risk management closely with Prologis' strategic objectives.

Under Bray's leadership, the risk management function at Prologis has evolved to incorporate cutting-edge methods such as catastrophe bonds and parametric insurance, enhancing the company's resilience and cost efficiency. The firm maintains a robust enterprise risk management framework that engages multiple departments, ensuring that risk considerations are integral to its decision-making processes. This collaborative approach reflects Prologis' commitment to embedding risk awareness across its global operations.

In addition to his corporate achievements, Bray is dedicated to nurturing the next generation of risk management professionals. He has actively participated in the Spencer Educational Foundation's Risk Manager in Residence program, fostering educational opportunities for students. His contributions, along with those of Emily Buckley, exemplify a commitment to the future of the profession, as noted by LaBranche when he stated that RISKWORLD will celebrate not only their achievements but also their generosity in sharing knowledge with emerging leaders.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Prologis, L.P. Launches C$850 Million Notes Offering Amid Strong Market Demand

Prologis, L.P. has announced the issuance of C$850 million in aggregate principal amount of 4.25% notes due 2034, reflecting a significant capital markets transaction for the logistics real estate giant. This offering is being underwritten by Scotia Capital Inc. and TD Securities Inc., and is poised to bolster the company's financial standing.

The transaction, dated 27th April 2026, is expected to generate net proceeds of approximately C$842.14 million before expenses. These funds will primarily be allocated for general corporate purposes, which include repaying existing borrowings under global credit lines and a Canadian dollar term loan. The move is anticipated to enhance Prologis' balance sheet, reducing short-term debt and interest expenses, thereby providing greater financial flexibility amidst a rising interest rate environment.

The notes will carry an interest rate of 4.25% per annum and are expected to be listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The offering's pricing reflects a spread of 102 basis points over the Government of Canada benchmark, indicative of robust demand for Prologis credit in Canadian dollars. This diversification in funding sources is deemed advantageous for the company's risk management strategy.

Prologis, L.P.'s underwriting agreement notes that there have been no material adverse changes in its business or financial condition since the last disclosures, which further reinforces investor confidence. The offering adheres to stringent regulatory requirements across multiple jurisdictions, ensuring compliance while providing the firm with a stable long-term debt profile that is likely to be viewed favorably by credit rating agencies.

As the settlement date for this transaction approaches, investors are encouraged to monitor Prologis for any updates regarding the utilization of proceeds and potential credit ratings actions. This offering is expected to positively influence the company's share price and credit spreads, particularly if accompanied by further balance sheet strengthening measures.

Thu Apr 23, 2026: ProLogis Expands Data Center Footprint As Demand Soars

ProLogis is making significant strides in the data center market as part of its strategic expansion efforts. The real estate giant has announced a major investment in data centers, aiming to capitalize on the growing demand for digital infrastructure. As of 23rd April 2026, this move positions ProLogis at the forefront of a sector that is witnessing unprecedented growth amidst an increasing reliance on cloud services and data processing capabilities.

The company has not only ramped up its investments but is also focusing on enhancing its existing data center portfolio. ProLogis aims to leverage its expertise in logistics and real estate to meet the burgeoning need for data storage and processing facilities. Industry experts suggest that this strategic pivot is timely, as enterprises are increasingly looking to bolster their digital operations and infrastructure.

ProLogis' foray into the data center market underscores a broader trend within the real estate sector, where traditional logistics firms are diversifying their portfolios to include tech-driven assets. The move is seen as a response to the rising demand for data processing capabilities, particularly as businesses expand their digital footprints. Analysts are closely monitoring ProLogis' efforts, as its success in this domain could set a benchmark for other companies.

With the growing emphasis on digital transformation across industries, ProLogis is well-positioned to benefit from this trend. The company's approach outlines a commitment to not only expand its reach but also to innovate within the data center space. As the competition heats up, ProLogis' investments may very well translate into substantial returns for investors looking for growth in the ever-evolving technology landscape.

In conclusion, ProLogis is strategically aligning itself with the future of real estate by investing heavily in data centers. This move not only reflects the company's forward-thinking approach but also signifies its adaptability to changing market demands. As the digital economy continues to expand, ProLogis is poised to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of data infrastructure.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE AND INDEPENDENT RATINGS

6.1 Activities

ProLogis Inc. is a global leader in logistics real estate, operating as a real estate investment trust (REIT) listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: PLD). The company's core business revolves around owning, developing, and managing a vast portfolio of industrial properties, including warehouses, distribution centers, and logistics facilities strategically located near major transportation hubs worldwide. These assets primarily serve e-commerce, manufacturing, and transportation sectors, facilitating efficient supply chain operations. The fastest-growing segments include e-commerce fulfillment centers and last-mile delivery infrastructure, fueled by the rapid expansion of online retail and global trade. Additionally, ProLogis is seeing strong growth in sustainable and technologically advanced properties, such as energy-efficient warehouses and data center-adjacent sites, to address rising demands for environmental responsibility and digital connectivity. With a presence in over 20 countries, the company leverages its expertise to deliver high-quality real estate solutions, supporting clients in optimizing their logistics networks.

ProLogis (NYSE:PLD) is a prominent player in the Prologis Europe logistics real estate market, driving growth through strategic ventures such as the Prologis pan-European JV and the Prologis La Caisse joint venture. With a robust Prologis development pipeline Europe, the company extends its influence by investing in Prologis modern distribution centers and Prologis sustainable logistics buildings. Notably, the Prologis €1B seed assets highlight the strength of its Prologis seed asset portfolio Europe. The Prologis institutional partnership La Caisse enhances its reach, supported by the Prologis strategic partnership La Caisse. NYSE:PLD Prologis news reflects its proactive expansion strategy, including Prologis European warehouse investment and Prologis logistics parks Europe. The Pan-European logistics joint venture and European warehouse JV €1B further exemplify its commitment to Prologis logistics property investment. Prologis e-commerce fulfillment centers Europe and Prologis last-mile logistics Europe underscore its innovation in Prologis industrial real estate Europe. With Prologis cold storage Europe and a Prologis logistics investment fund Europe, ProLogis is well-positioned for future success.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN FRANCISCO, Wednesday, May 06 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, ProLogis has shown some strength in recent days. ProLogis (NYSE:PLD), lifted 1.9% during its previous trading session to close at $142.90.

ProLogis, Inc. (NYSE:PLD) is a leading global provider of logistics real estate solutions, strategically enhancing supply chain efficiency through innovative industrial properties. With an extensive portfolio including logistics parks like Prologis Park Sumner and North Chennai logistics park, the company demonstrates strong market presence in industrial real estate. It is actively developing sustainable logistics solutions and expanding its renewable energy portfolio in collaboration with partners such as the SolarCycle partnership. Prologis's competitive strengths lie in its scale and strategic joint ventures, such as the €1B seed assets JV. Its adaptation to regional needs, evidenced by initiatives like Prologis India development projects, underscores its commitment to long-term growth.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Prologis’ Jeff Bray Honored as 2026 Risk Manager of the Year

RIMS has named Jeff Bray, senior vice president and head of global risk management at Prologis, as the 2026 Risk Manager of the Year. The announcement comes as RIMS prepares for the RISKWORLD conference in Philadelphia next month, where Bray will receive his award. The date of the announcement, 27th April 2026, highlights the ongoing commitment of RIMS to recognize leaders in the risk management profession.

The Risk Manager of the Year program, which began in 1977, is designed to elevate the profile of the risk management profession by spotlighting exceptional contributions made by individuals within their respective organizations. Emily Buckley, insurance risk manager for Specialized Bicycle Components, has also been honored with induction into the Risk Management Honor Roll. RIMS CEO Gary LaBranche remarked, "The best leaders in this field don't just protect value, they help create it," underscoring the impact of Bray's innovative strategies at Prologis.

Prologis, headquartered in San Francisco, is a leading player in logistics real estate, managing approximately 1.3 billion square feet across 20 countries. Since his tenure began at AMB Property Corporation in 2005 and subsequent leadership role post-AMB's merger with Prologis in 2011, Bray has developed a comprehensive global risk management framework. His approach integrates insurance programs, claims management, and strategies for addressing emerging risks, thus aligning risk management closely with Prologis' strategic objectives.

Under Bray's leadership, the risk management function at Prologis has evolved to incorporate cutting-edge methods such as catastrophe bonds and parametric insurance, enhancing the company's resilience and cost efficiency. The firm maintains a robust enterprise risk management framework that engages multiple departments, ensuring that risk considerations are integral to its decision-making processes. This collaborative approach reflects Prologis' commitment to embedding risk awareness across its global operations.

In addition to his corporate achievements, Bray is dedicated to nurturing the next generation of risk management professionals. He has actively participated in the Spencer Educational Foundation's Risk Manager in Residence program, fostering educational opportunities for students. His contributions, along with those of Emily Buckley, exemplify a commitment to the future of the profession, as noted by LaBranche when he stated that RISKWORLD will celebrate not only their achievements but also their generosity in sharing knowledge with emerging leaders.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 295 words

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Prologis, L.P. Launches C$850 Million Notes Offering Amid Strong Market Demand

Prologis, L.P. has announced the issuance of C$850 million in aggregate principal amount of 4.25% notes due 2034, reflecting a significant capital markets transaction for the logistics real estate giant. This offering is being underwritten by Scotia Capital Inc. and TD Securities Inc., and is poised to bolster the company's financial standing.

The transaction, dated 27th April 2026, is expected to generate net proceeds of approximately C$842.14 million before expenses. These funds will primarily be allocated for general corporate purposes, which include repaying existing borrowings under global credit lines and a Canadian dollar term loan. The move is anticipated to enhance Prologis' balance sheet, reducing short-term debt and interest expenses, thereby providing greater financial flexibility amidst a rising interest rate environment.

The notes will carry an interest rate of 4.25% per annum and are expected to be listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The offering's pricing reflects a spread of 102 basis points over the Government of Canada benchmark, indicative of robust demand for Prologis credit in Canadian dollars. This diversification in funding sources is deemed advantageous for the company's risk management strategy.

Prologis, L.P.'s underwriting agreement notes that there have been no material adverse changes in its business or financial condition since the last disclosures, which further reinforces investor confidence. The offering adheres to stringent regulatory requirements across multiple jurisdictions, ensuring compliance while providing the firm with a stable long-term debt profile that is likely to be viewed favorably by credit rating agencies.

As the settlement date for this transaction approaches, investors are encouraged to monitor Prologis for any updates regarding the utilization of proceeds and potential credit ratings actions. This offering is expected to positively influence the company's share price and credit spreads, particularly if accompanied by further balance sheet strengthening measures.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 323 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: ProLogis Expands Data Center Footprint As Demand Soars

ProLogis is making significant strides in the data center market as part of its strategic expansion efforts. The real estate giant has announced a major investment in data centers, aiming to capitalize on the growing demand for digital infrastructure. As of 23rd April 2026, this move positions ProLogis at the forefront of a sector that is witnessing unprecedented growth amidst an increasing reliance on cloud services and data processing capabilities.

The company has not only ramped up its investments but is also focusing on enhancing its existing data center portfolio. ProLogis aims to leverage its expertise in logistics and real estate to meet the burgeoning need for data storage and processing facilities. Industry experts suggest that this strategic pivot is timely, as enterprises are increasingly looking to bolster their digital operations and infrastructure.

ProLogis' foray into the data center market underscores a broader trend within the real estate sector, where traditional logistics firms are diversifying their portfolios to include tech-driven assets. The move is seen as a response to the rising demand for data processing capabilities, particularly as businesses expand their digital footprints. Analysts are closely monitoring ProLogis' efforts, as its success in this domain could set a benchmark for other companies.

With the growing emphasis on digital transformation across industries, ProLogis is well-positioned to benefit from this trend. The company's approach outlines a commitment to not only expand its reach but also to innovate within the data center space. As the competition heats up, ProLogis' investments may very well translate into substantial returns for investors looking for growth in the ever-evolving technology landscape.

In conclusion, ProLogis is strategically aligning itself with the future of real estate by investing heavily in data centers. This move not only reflects the company's forward-thinking approach but also signifies its adaptability to changing market demands. As the digital economy continues to expand, ProLogis is poised to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of data infrastructure.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE AND INDEPENDENT RATINGS

6.1 Activities

ProLogis Inc. is a global leader in logistics real estate, operating as a real estate investment trust (REIT) listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: PLD). The company's core business revolves around owning, developing, and managing a vast portfolio of industrial properties, including warehouses, distribution centers, and logistics facilities strategically located near major transportation hubs worldwide. These assets primarily serve e-commerce, manufacturing, and transportation sectors, facilitating efficient supply chain operations. The fastest-growing segments include e-commerce fulfillment centers and last-mile delivery infrastructure, fueled by the rapid expansion of online retail and global trade. Additionally, ProLogis is seeing strong growth in sustainable and technologically advanced properties, such as energy-efficient warehouses and data center-adjacent sites, to address rising demands for environmental responsibility and digital connectivity. With a presence in over 20 countries, the company leverages its expertise to deliver high-quality real estate solutions, supporting clients in optimizing their logistics networks.

ProLogis (NYSE:PLD) is a prominent player in the Prologis Europe logistics real estate market, driving growth through strategic ventures such as the Prologis pan-European JV and the Prologis La Caisse joint venture. With a robust Prologis development pipeline Europe, the company extends its influence by investing in Prologis modern distribution centers and Prologis sustainable logistics buildings. Notably, the Prologis €1B seed assets highlight the strength of its Prologis seed asset portfolio Europe. The Prologis institutional partnership La Caisse enhances its reach, supported by the Prologis strategic partnership La Caisse. NYSE:PLD Prologis news reflects its proactive expansion strategy, including Prologis European warehouse investment and Prologis logistics parks Europe. The Pan-European logistics joint venture and European warehouse JV €1B further exemplify its commitment to Prologis logistics property investment. Prologis e-commerce fulfillment centers Europe and Prologis last-mile logistics Europe underscore its innovation in Prologis industrial real estate Europe. With Prologis cold storage Europe and a Prologis logistics investment fund Europe, ProLogis is well-positioned for future success.

urn:contentItem:6JH5-WKW3-S8J4-84P9-00000-00Reviewing Enovix (NASDAQ:ENVX) and Montauk Renewables (NASDAQ:MNTK)2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisReviewing Enovix (NASDAQ:ENVX) and Montauk Renewables (NASDAQ:MNTK)

May 4th, 2026 ( Zolmax.com - Delivered by Newstex )

Montauk Renewables ( NASDAQ:MNTK - Get Free Report) and Enovix ( NASDAQ:ENVX - Get Free Report) are both small-cap energy companies, but which is the better stock? We will compare the two businesses based on the strength of their profitability, risk, earnings, dividends, analyst recommendations, institutional ownership and valuation.

Valuation & Earnings

This table compares Montauk Renewables and Enovix"s revenue, earnings per share (EPS) and valuation.

Montauk Renewables has higher revenue and earnings than Enovix. Enovix is trading at a lower price-to-earnings ratio than Montauk Renewables, indicating that it is currently the more affordable of the two stocks.

Volatility and Risk

Montauk Renewables has a beta of 0.47, suggesting that its stock price is 53% less volatile than the S&P 500. Comparatively, Enovix has a beta of 2.24, suggesting that its stock price is 124% more volatile than the S&P 500.

Institutional & Insider Ownership

16.4% of Montauk Renewables shares are held by institutional investors. Comparatively, 50.9% of Enovix shares are held by institutional investors. 54.3% of Montauk Renewables shares are held by company insiders. Comparatively, 12.7% of Enovix shares are held by company insiders. Strong institutional ownership is an indication that hedge funds, endowments and large money managers believe a company will outperform the market over the long term.

Analyst Ratings

This is a breakdown of recent ratings and target prices for Montauk Renewables and Enovix, as provided by MarketBeat.com.

Montauk Renewables presently has a consensus price target of $1.80, suggesting a potential upside of 31.39%. Enovix has a consensus price target of $14.05, suggesting a potential upside of 108.08%. Given Enovix's stronger consensus rating and higher possible upside, analysts plainly believe Enovix is more favorable than Montauk Renewables.

Profitability

This table compares Montauk Renewables and Enovix's net margins, return on equity and return on assets.

Summary

Montauk Renewables beats Enovix on 9 of the 15 factors compared between the two stocks.

About Montauk Renewables

( Get Free Report)

Montauk Renewables, Inc., a renewable energy company, engages in recovery and processing of biogas from landfills and other non-fossil fuel sources. It operates in two segments, Renewable Natural Gas and Renewable Electricity Generation. The company develops, owns, and operates renewable natural gas (RNG) projects that captures methane and prevents it from being released into the atmosphere by converting it into either RNG or electrical power for the electrical grid. Its customers for RNG and renewable identification numbers (RIN) include large, long-term owner-operators of landfills and livestock farms, local utilities, and large refiners in the natural gas and refining sectors. Montauk Renewables, Inc. was founded in 1980 and is headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.

About Enovix

( Get Free Report)

Enovix Corporation designs develops and manufactures silicon-anode lithium-ion batteries. It serves wearables and IoT, smartphone, laptops and tablets, industrial and medical, and electric vehicles industries. The company was founded in 2007 and is headquartered in Fremont, California.

value.Title: Pa. House advances measure to develop, regulate geothermal energy industry

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-BCP3-RWR6-04H8-00000-00Pa. House advances measure to develop, regulate geothermal energy industry2026-05-28T16:10:31Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisCNHI NETWORKPa. House advances measure to develop, regulate geothermal energy industryEric Scicchitano

HARRISBURG The potential development of geothermal energy in Pennsylvania got a lift Monday as members of the state House voted to advance legislation proposing a regulatory framework for the energy source.

Pennsylvania hasn't been viewed as a potential site for large-scale geothermal energy; however, the evolution of deep drilling thousands of feet below ground within the natural gas industry has made geothermal potentially viable in Pennsylvania beyond shallow-depth heat pumps, according to the independent and legislative agency reports.

Now, the commonwealth is hosting the first pilot project of its kind in the eastern United States to determine just how viable geothermal energy could be. The federal government awarded a $14 million grant to Pennsylvania earlier this month in support of the pilot project in Indiana County. Electricity and thermal energy generated through the project will be used to heat local homes and businesses near the project site, with excess electricity redirected to the power grid.

Rep. Arvind Venkat, D-Allegheny, who introduced House Bill 2076, said the bill not only sets up the framework but also defines property rights and creates a path to repurpose abandoned oil and gas wells.

Pennsylvania has an estimated 760,000 abandoned wells, some more than 100 years old, that pose environmental hazards.

"With next-generation enhanced geothermal systems, the opportunity is here now in Pennsylvania to develop this energy source," Venkat said.

House members voted 118-83 to advance the bill, with 16 Republicans joining all 102 Democrats in support. It now moves to the state Senate for further consideration by an upper chamber deeply interested in energy development.

Geothermal energy is considered renewable, sustainable and constantly available. Its potential for generating electricity, creating heat and also as a subsurface cooling resource could be important for the development of high-energy, water-needy hyperscale data centers, both for power and cooling.

A report from 2025 that supports geothermal energy in Pennsylvania says that with the right policy and regulatory action, the commonwealth could swiftly develop the industry and infrastructure in concert with abandoned and operating oil and gas interests, including their skilled workforce.

According to the bill, rights to geothermal resources below ground would be considered the property of those who own the surface above, allowing them to convey those rights to companies seeking to extract the resources.

The bill includes provisions that geothermal projects be isolated from underground coal, oil and gas reserves that are commercially viable. Owners of subsurface mineral rights could object to geothermal projects if they believe their interests are being infringed.

The legislation would authorize DEP to oversee geothermal development and project permitting, guided by regulations promulgated by the Environmental Quality Board that would determine, for example, the use of injection well fluids, appropriate project siting and production rates, setbacks and bonding requirements.

The bill's current language dictates that bonds won't exceed those currently required for conventional, unconventional and injection wells unless deemed necessary to maintain oversight and enforcement based on a specific well's characteristics.

urn:contentItem:6JGF-P4N3-RXSN-X26G-00000-00Report: Carbon taxes cost Pennsylvania $5B2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:08:35ZLexisNexisENVIRONMENT NEWS & CLIMATE CHANGE NEWSReport: Carbon taxes cost Pennsylvania $5BBy Christen Smith | The Center Square

(The Center Square) - Energy is big business in Pennsylvania.

Its storied fossil fuel reserves have kept utility bills lower and the regional power grid more stable for the better part of a century.

That's remained key as states opt for lower-emission power sources, which have so far relied on fledgling technologies not yet able to keep the lights on with batteries, solar panels and wind turbines alone.

A new report from the Commonwealth Foundation concludes it could have been even bigger business had state regulators not spent the last six years "flirting" with a carbon tax program called the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative, the success of which many critics say relies on the fossil fuels it means to curb.

For the program's most ardent supporters, the transition away from coal and gas must start somewhere. And, to them, a 50% reduction in emissions and $6 billion in revenue over the last 18 years isn't a bad one.

"The Keystone State's six-year on-again, off-again relationship with RGGI is a cautionary tale for other states, such as Virginia," said Elizabeth Stelle, vice president of policy for the Commonwealth Foundation. "RGGI is a carbon tax in sheep's clothing, toppling the energy future of every state it touches."

RGGI, as the program is often referred to, profits from reductions in harmful emissions by charging generators for the air pollution they create, measured in carbon dioxide. Across 12 states in the mid-Atlantic and New England, power plants and industrial sites buy an ever-decreasing number of emissions "credits" at the program's annual auction to cover that anticipated pollution.

Those credit prices have risen sharply since RGGI's first auction in 2008, and because of the balance needed between fossil fuels and renewable power, the program has put financial pressure on the entire grid, leaving utility companies - and by extension, ratepayers - scrambling to make up the difference.

Sometimes relief comes from RGGI-funded bill-reduction programs administered by participating states. Or the proliferation of more efficient renewable sources, supported by government taxpayer-funded subsidies, that require less support from gas and coal. In recent years, it's been the pooling of smart thermostats, rooftop solar panels, and battery storage to compensate consumers for lower electricity usage.

And yes, it's been rate increases too. But if not for Pennsylvania, where 25% of the regional power grid's electricity is sourced, conventional wisdom has long held that those hikes could have been far steeper.

Which is why former two-term Democratic Gov. Tom Wolf's 2019 executive order adding Pennsylvania to the RGGI program triggered a six-year regulatory war with lawmakers to stop it before it ever happened.

The foundation, a conservative policy group based in Harrisburg, was among the loudest critics - behind legislative Republicans, with whom the organization typically aligns - of the proposal, citing quadrupled energy costs and a $5 billion investment shortfall, according to its report.

In terms of raw numbers, the chilling effect of potentially joining RGGI is stark. Between 2013 and 2018, 73% of proposed power projects in the commonwealth became operational. After regulators set their sights on RGGI in 2019, that conversion rate collapsed to 9%.

By comparison, the rate for Ohio - a non-RGGI state with a wealth of fossil fuel plants - declined much more slowly, from 62% to 48%, in part due to falling gas prices.

"Unfortunately, that loss is not shocking," Stelle said. "While Pennsylvania debated, litigated, and delayed over carbon tax policy, Ohio provided developers with a predictable regulatory environment to commit billions in capital. Projects that might have started in Pennsylvania went to Ohio or never came to fruition."

Losing to "friggin' Ohio" has jokingly been a thorn in the side of first-term Democratic Gov. Josh Shapiro since he took office in 2023, even calling out the Buckeye State's competitive economic policies during his first budget address to the General Assembly.

Shapiro, on the campaign trail, expressed his doubts about RGGI, though he continued the Wolf administration's legal fight to make Pennsylvania participate for two more years, only dropping it as part of a four-month-late budget deal this past November.

RGGI detractors say taxing generators would not just make it less profitable to build new power plants. It would raise energy costs for everyone by accelerating the closure of long-serving facilities, reducing supply, and raising electricity prices.

Shapiro, too, worries about affordability and instability, though he's pointed the finger at PJM, the organization that manages the power grid, and at its lengthy planning process. He even filed complaints with federal regulators to force the organization to lower electricity price caps, temporarily preventing a 30% spike for ratepayers.

He's even threatened, more than once, to decouple the state from the grid and "go it alone" - an unprecedented move that could undo a century of cooperation.

It hasn't come to that yet. Instead, Shapiro has lobbied the General Assembly to act on his state-centric Lightning Plan to quadruple wind and solar power over the decade - and still limit emissions from larger plants.

Stelle said doing so would double residential electricity bills.

"Affordable, reliable energy is not achieved through Green New Deal-style mandates and burdensome regulation," she said. "It happens naturally when we pursue policies that welcome the investment its grid desperately needs, letting Pennsylvania's rich energy sector do what it does best."

In the eyes of some, air pollution is what the energy industry does best. In 2020, federal data rankedPennsylvania fourth nationwide for carbon emissions, and estimates suggest that 1% of global greenhouse gas pollution comes from the commonwealth alone.

Supporters of programs like RGGI often point to the dual impacts of reduced emissions and investment in renewable technology as key to their value for an energy-rich state like Pennsylvania.

A 2023 report from the Kleinman Center for Energy Policy at the University of Pennsylvania concluded that RGGI participation would have reduced the state's electricity-sector emissions by 84% in 2030, compared to roughly 52% without it.

It could have been achieved without raising utility bills or decimating energy exports, according to the report. Researchers said wind and solar generation would have softened the impact of lost natural gas production and retail electricity prices will actually decline 0.6% by the time the RGGI cap zeroes out in 2040.

Shapiro's state-based plans - the Pennsylvania Reliable Energy Sustainability Standard and the Pennsylvania Climate Emissions Reduction Act, PRESS and PACER for short - similarly broaden the mix of energy providers so that the power grid isn't sustained primarily by fossil fuels.

This time, however, 70% of the money recovered from state-imposed emissions caps would support bill-reduction programs.

And, based on modeling from Synapse Energy Economics that was commissioned by the state Department of Environmental Protection last year, it would lower residential bills by $1 per month.

What's not entirely clear is how rapid data center development would collide with such policy, if it were to be implemented. More than 50 proposals are pending across the state, and their economic viability could falter if the state recommits to carbon taxing programs.

urn:contentItem:6JJF-N343-RTK4-D082-00000-00Can ProLogis (NYSE:PLD), turn momentum into continued success?2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisCan ProLogis (NYSE:PLD), turn momentum into continued success?

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN FRANCISCO, Friday, May 08 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, ProLogis closed higher the previous trading day. ProLogis (NYSE:PLD), lifted 1.3% during its previous trading session to close at $144.09.

ProLogis, Inc. (NYSE:PLD) is a leading global provider of logistics real estate solutions, strategically enhancing supply chain efficiency through innovative industrial properties. With an extensive portfolio including logistics parks like Prologis Park Sumner and North Chennai logistics park, the company demonstrates strong market presence in industrial real estate. It is actively developing sustainable logistics solutions and expanding its renewable energy portfolio in collaboration with partners such as the SolarCycle partnership. Prologis's competitive strengths lie in its scale and strategic joint ventures, such as the €1B seed assets JV. Its adaptation to regional needs, evidenced by initiatives like Prologis India development projects, underscores its commitment to long-term growth.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Prologis’ Jeff Bray Honored as 2026 Risk Manager of the Year

RIMS has named Jeff Bray, senior vice president and head of global risk management at Prologis, as the 2026 Risk Manager of the Year. The announcement comes as RIMS prepares for the RISKWORLD conference in Philadelphia next month, where Bray will receive his award. The date of the announcement, 27th April 2026, highlights the ongoing commitment of RIMS to recognize leaders in the risk management profession.

The Risk Manager of the Year program, which began in 1977, is designed to elevate the profile of the risk management profession by spotlighting exceptional contributions made by individuals within their respective organizations. Emily Buckley, insurance risk manager for Specialized Bicycle Components, has also been honored with induction into the Risk Management Honor Roll. RIMS CEO Gary LaBranche remarked, "The best leaders in this field don't just protect value, they help create it," underscoring the impact of Bray's innovative strategies at Prologis.

Prologis, headquartered in San Francisco, is a leading player in logistics real estate, managing approximately 1.3 billion square feet across 20 countries. Since his tenure began at AMB Property Corporation in 2005 and subsequent leadership role post-AMB's merger with Prologis in 2011, Bray has developed a comprehensive global risk management framework. His approach integrates insurance programs, claims management, and strategies for addressing emerging risks, thus aligning risk management closely with Prologis' strategic objectives.

Under Bray's leadership, the risk management function at Prologis has evolved to incorporate cutting-edge methods such as catastrophe bonds and parametric insurance, enhancing the company's resilience and cost efficiency. The firm maintains a robust enterprise risk management framework that engages multiple departments, ensuring that risk considerations are integral to its decision-making processes. This collaborative approach reflects Prologis' commitment to embedding risk awareness across its global operations.

In addition to his corporate achievements, Bray is dedicated to nurturing the next generation of risk management professionals. He has actively participated in the Spencer Educational Foundation's Risk Manager in Residence program, fostering educational opportunities for students. His contributions, along with those of Emily Buckley, exemplify a commitment to the future of the profession, as noted by LaBranche when he stated that RISKWORLD will celebrate not only their achievements but also their generosity in sharing knowledge with emerging leaders.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 295 words

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Prologis, L.P. Launches C$850 Million Notes Offering Amid Strong Market Demand

Prologis, L.P. has announced the issuance of C$850 million in aggregate principal amount of 4.25% notes due 2034, reflecting a significant capital markets transaction for the logistics real estate giant. This offering is being underwritten by Scotia Capital Inc. and TD Securities Inc., and is poised to bolster the company's financial standing.

The transaction, dated 27th April 2026, is expected to generate net proceeds of approximately C$842.14 million before expenses. These funds will primarily be allocated for general corporate purposes, which include repaying existing borrowings under global credit lines and a Canadian dollar term loan. The move is anticipated to enhance Prologis' balance sheet, reducing short-term debt and interest expenses, thereby providing greater financial flexibility amidst a rising interest rate environment.

The notes will carry an interest rate of 4.25% per annum and are expected to be listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The offering's pricing reflects a spread of 102 basis points over the Government of Canada benchmark, indicative of robust demand for Prologis credit in Canadian dollars. This diversification in funding sources is deemed advantageous for the company's risk management strategy.

Prologis, L.P.'s underwriting agreement notes that there have been no material adverse changes in its business or financial condition since the last disclosures, which further reinforces investor confidence. The offering adheres to stringent regulatory requirements across multiple jurisdictions, ensuring compliance while providing the firm with a stable long-term debt profile that is likely to be viewed favorably by credit rating agencies.

As the settlement date for this transaction approaches, investors are encouraged to monitor Prologis for any updates regarding the utilization of proceeds and potential credit ratings actions. This offering is expected to positively influence the company's share price and credit spreads, particularly if accompanied by further balance sheet strengthening measures.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 323 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: ProLogis Expands Data Center Footprint As Demand Soars

ProLogis is making significant strides in the data center market as part of its strategic expansion efforts. The real estate giant has announced a major investment in data centers, aiming to capitalize on the growing demand for digital infrastructure. As of 23rd April 2026, this move positions ProLogis at the forefront of a sector that is witnessing unprecedented growth amidst an increasing reliance on cloud services and data processing capabilities.

The company has not only ramped up its investments but is also focusing on enhancing its existing data center portfolio. ProLogis aims to leverage its expertise in logistics and real estate to meet the burgeoning need for data storage and processing facilities. Industry experts suggest that this strategic pivot is timely, as enterprises are increasingly looking to bolster their digital operations and infrastructure.

ProLogis' foray into the data center market underscores a broader trend within the real estate sector, where traditional logistics firms are diversifying their portfolios to include tech-driven assets. The move is seen as a response to the rising demand for data processing capabilities, particularly as businesses expand their digital footprints. Analysts are closely monitoring ProLogis' efforts, as its success in this domain could set a benchmark for other companies.

With the growing emphasis on digital transformation across industries, ProLogis is well-positioned to benefit from this trend. The company's approach outlines a commitment to not only expand its reach but also to innovate within the data center space. As the competition heats up, ProLogis' investments may very well translate into substantial returns for investors looking for growth in the ever-evolving technology landscape.

In conclusion, ProLogis is strategically aligning itself with the future of real estate by investing heavily in data centers. This move not only reflects the company's forward-thinking approach but also signifies its adaptability to changing market demands. As the digital economy continues to expand, ProLogis is poised to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of data infrastructure.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE AND INDEPENDENT RATINGS

6.1 Activities

ProLogis Inc. is a global leader in logistics real estate, operating as a real estate investment trust (REIT) listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: PLD). The company's core business revolves around owning, developing, and managing a vast portfolio of industrial properties, including warehouses, distribution centers, and logistics facilities strategically located near major transportation hubs worldwide. These assets primarily serve e-commerce, manufacturing, and transportation sectors, facilitating efficient supply chain operations. The fastest-growing segments include e-commerce fulfillment centers and last-mile delivery infrastructure, fueled by the rapid expansion of online retail and global trade. Additionally, ProLogis is seeing strong growth in sustainable and technologically advanced properties, such as energy-efficient warehouses and data center-adjacent sites, to address rising demands for environmental responsibility and digital connectivity. With a presence in over 20 countries, the company leverages its expertise to deliver high-quality real estate solutions, supporting clients in optimizing their logistics networks.

ProLogis (NYSE:PLD) is a prominent player in the Prologis Europe logistics real estate market, driving growth through strategic ventures such as the Prologis pan-European JV and the Prologis La Caisse joint venture. With a robust Prologis development pipeline Europe, the company extends its influence by investing in Prologis modern distribution centers and Prologis sustainable logistics buildings. Notably, the Prologis €1B seed assets highlight the strength of its Prologis seed asset portfolio Europe. The Prologis institutional partnership La Caisse enhances its reach, supported by the Prologis strategic partnership La Caisse. NYSE:PLD Prologis news reflects its proactive expansion strategy, including Prologis European warehouse investment and Prologis logistics parks Europe. The Pan-European logistics joint venture and European warehouse JV €1B further exemplify its commitment to Prologis logistics property investment. Prologis e-commerce fulfillment centers Europe and Prologis last-mile logistics Europe underscore its innovation in Prologis industrial real estate Europe. With Prologis cold storage Europe and a Prologis logistics investment fund Europe, ProLogis is well-positioned for future success.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

SAN FRANCISCO, Friday, May 08 2026 - Ahead of this morning's market opening, ProLogis closed higher the previous trading day. ProLogis (NYSE:PLD), lifted 1.3% during its previous trading session to close at $144.09.

ProLogis, Inc. (NYSE:PLD) is a leading global provider of logistics real estate solutions, strategically enhancing supply chain efficiency through innovative industrial properties. With an extensive portfolio including logistics parks like Prologis Park Sumner and North Chennai logistics park, the company demonstrates strong market presence in industrial real estate. It is actively developing sustainable logistics solutions and expanding its renewable energy portfolio in collaboration with partners such as the SolarCycle partnership. Prologis's competitive strengths lie in its scale and strategic joint ventures, such as the €1B seed assets JV. Its adaptation to regional needs, evidenced by initiatives like Prologis India development projects, underscores its commitment to long-term growth.

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Prologis’ Jeff Bray Honored as 2026 Risk Manager of the Year

RIMS has named Jeff Bray, senior vice president and head of global risk management at Prologis, as the 2026 Risk Manager of the Year. The announcement comes as RIMS prepares for the RISKWORLD conference in Philadelphia next month, where Bray will receive his award. The date of the announcement, 27th April 2026, highlights the ongoing commitment of RIMS to recognize leaders in the risk management profession.

The Risk Manager of the Year program, which began in 1977, is designed to elevate the profile of the risk management profession by spotlighting exceptional contributions made by individuals within their respective organizations. Emily Buckley, insurance risk manager for Specialized Bicycle Components, has also been honored with induction into the Risk Management Honor Roll. RIMS CEO Gary LaBranche remarked, "The best leaders in this field don't just protect value, they help create it," underscoring the impact of Bray's innovative strategies at Prologis.

Prologis, headquartered in San Francisco, is a leading player in logistics real estate, managing approximately 1.3 billion square feet across 20 countries. Since his tenure began at AMB Property Corporation in 2005 and subsequent leadership role post-AMB's merger with Prologis in 2011, Bray has developed a comprehensive global risk management framework. His approach integrates insurance programs, claims management, and strategies for addressing emerging risks, thus aligning risk management closely with Prologis' strategic objectives.

Under Bray's leadership, the risk management function at Prologis has evolved to incorporate cutting-edge methods such as catastrophe bonds and parametric insurance, enhancing the company's resilience and cost efficiency. The firm maintains a robust enterprise risk management framework that engages multiple departments, ensuring that risk considerations are integral to its decision-making processes. This collaborative approach reflects Prologis' commitment to embedding risk awareness across its global operations.

In addition to his corporate achievements, Bray is dedicated to nurturing the next generation of risk management professionals. He has actively participated in the Spencer Educational Foundation's Risk Manager in Residence program, fostering educational opportunities for students. His contributions, along with those of Emily Buckley, exemplify a commitment to the future of the profession, as noted by LaBranche when he stated that RISKWORLD will celebrate not only their achievements but also their generosity in sharing knowledge with emerging leaders.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 295 words

Mon Apr 27, 2026: Prologis, L.P. Launches C$850 Million Notes Offering Amid Strong Market Demand

Prologis, L.P. has announced the issuance of C$850 million in aggregate principal amount of 4.25% notes due 2034, reflecting a significant capital markets transaction for the logistics real estate giant. This offering is being underwritten by Scotia Capital Inc. and TD Securities Inc., and is poised to bolster the company's financial standing.

The transaction, dated 27th April 2026, is expected to generate net proceeds of approximately C$842.14 million before expenses. These funds will primarily be allocated for general corporate purposes, which include repaying existing borrowings under global credit lines and a Canadian dollar term loan. The move is anticipated to enhance Prologis' balance sheet, reducing short-term debt and interest expenses, thereby providing greater financial flexibility amidst a rising interest rate environment.

The notes will carry an interest rate of 4.25% per annum and are expected to be listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The offering's pricing reflects a spread of 102 basis points over the Government of Canada benchmark, indicative of robust demand for Prologis credit in Canadian dollars. This diversification in funding sources is deemed advantageous for the company's risk management strategy.

Prologis, L.P.'s underwriting agreement notes that there have been no material adverse changes in its business or financial condition since the last disclosures, which further reinforces investor confidence. The offering adheres to stringent regulatory requirements across multiple jurisdictions, ensuring compliance while providing the firm with a stable long-term debt profile that is likely to be viewed favorably by credit rating agencies.

As the settlement date for this transaction approaches, investors are encouraged to monitor Prologis for any updates regarding the utilization of proceeds and potential credit ratings actions. This offering is expected to positively influence the company's share price and credit spreads, particularly if accompanied by further balance sheet strengthening measures.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 323 words

Thu Apr 23, 2026: ProLogis Expands Data Center Footprint As Demand Soars

ProLogis is making significant strides in the data center market as part of its strategic expansion efforts. The real estate giant has announced a major investment in data centers, aiming to capitalize on the growing demand for digital infrastructure. As of 23rd April 2026, this move positions ProLogis at the forefront of a sector that is witnessing unprecedented growth amidst an increasing reliance on cloud services and data processing capabilities.

The company has not only ramped up its investments but is also focusing on enhancing its existing data center portfolio. ProLogis aims to leverage its expertise in logistics and real estate to meet the burgeoning need for data storage and processing facilities. Industry experts suggest that this strategic pivot is timely, as enterprises are increasingly looking to bolster their digital operations and infrastructure.

ProLogis' foray into the data center market underscores a broader trend within the real estate sector, where traditional logistics firms are diversifying their portfolios to include tech-driven assets. The move is seen as a response to the rising demand for data processing capabilities, particularly as businesses expand their digital footprints. Analysts are closely monitoring ProLogis' efforts, as its success in this domain could set a benchmark for other companies.

With the growing emphasis on digital transformation across industries, ProLogis is well-positioned to benefit from this trend. The company's approach outlines a commitment to not only expand its reach but also to innovate within the data center space. As the competition heats up, ProLogis' investments may very well translate into substantial returns for investors looking for growth in the ever-evolving technology landscape.

In conclusion, ProLogis is strategically aligning itself with the future of real estate by investing heavily in data centers. This move not only reflects the company's forward-thinking approach but also signifies its adaptability to changing market demands. As the digital economy continues to expand, ProLogis is poised to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of data infrastructure.

SECTION 6 CORPORATE PROFILE AND INDEPENDENT RATINGS

6.1 Activities

ProLogis Inc. is a global leader in logistics real estate, operating as a real estate investment trust (REIT) listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: PLD). The company's core business revolves around owning, developing, and managing a vast portfolio of industrial properties, including warehouses, distribution centers, and logistics facilities strategically located near major transportation hubs worldwide. These assets primarily serve e-commerce, manufacturing, and transportation sectors, facilitating efficient supply chain operations. The fastest-growing segments include e-commerce fulfillment centers and last-mile delivery infrastructure, fueled by the rapid expansion of online retail and global trade. Additionally, ProLogis is seeing strong growth in sustainable and technologically advanced properties, such as energy-efficient warehouses and data center-adjacent sites, to address rising demands for environmental responsibility and digital connectivity. With a presence in over 20 countries, the company leverages its expertise to deliver high-quality real estate solutions, supporting clients in optimizing their logistics networks.

ProLogis (NYSE:PLD) is a prominent player in the Prologis Europe logistics real estate market, driving growth through strategic ventures such as the Prologis pan-European JV and the Prologis La Caisse joint venture. With a robust Prologis development pipeline Europe, the company extends its influence by investing in Prologis modern distribution centers and Prologis sustainable logistics buildings. Notably, the Prologis €1B seed assets highlight the strength of its Prologis seed asset portfolio Europe. The Prologis institutional partnership La Caisse enhances its reach, supported by the Prologis strategic partnership La Caisse. NYSE:PLD Prologis news reflects its proactive expansion strategy, including Prologis European warehouse investment and Prologis logistics parks Europe. The Pan-European logistics joint venture and European warehouse JV €1B further exemplify its commitment to Prologis logistics property investment. Prologis e-commerce fulfillment centers Europe and Prologis last-mile logistics Europe underscore its innovation in Prologis industrial real estate Europe. With Prologis cold storage Europe and a Prologis logistics investment fund Europe, ProLogis is well-positioned for future success.

value.Title: May 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. & PEER INSIGHTS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ7-P0T3-S31K-S1WD-00000-00May 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. & PEER INSIGHTS2026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 09, 2026: TRACKING TRENDS: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO. & PEER INSIGHTS

Consigli Construction Co. is making significant strides with its recent involvement in the Williams College Museum of Art project, marking the museum's first purpose-built facility on campus. Set to begin on February 25, 2026, the project presents a unique design challenge that aims to enhance student access to art collections. Abigail Berkebile, a prominent figure at Consigli Construction Co., highlighted the project's complexity and the rewarding nature of collaborating with the museum staff. In conjunction with this development, Berkebile emphasized the vital role of women in the construction industry, noting that opportunities extend far beyond traditional field positions to include accounting, IT, legal, marketing, and design. By maintaining a structured approach to daily team collaboration, Consigli Construction Co. continues to focus on executing complex institutional builds with a diverse array of professional expertise.

In the broader sector, Suffolk Construction is advancing critical infrastructure and renewable energy initiatives. On May 5, 2026, the Virginia Department of Transportation is scheduled to lift a long-term lane closure on U.S. Route 58 westbound near downtown Suffolk, a change tied to the ongoing Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project managed by the firm. The project will ultimately provide a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic to access a regional landfill. Concurrently, Virgin Media O2 announced a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement on May 1, 2026, to source energy from a 70MW solar farm in Suffolk, which is currently under construction and expected to commence power generation in 2027. This solar initiative is projected to supply approximately five percent of the telecommunications company's total energy demands.

Turner Construction is also marking major structural milestones across multiple large-scale developments. On April 28, 2026, the company celebrated the topping out of the 72-megawatt Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, a 450,000-square-foot facility featuring a hybrid cooling solution. Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction, credited the milestone to strong collaboration with Aligned and the dedication of approximately 345 skilled workers logging over 80,000 craft hours. On the same day, Turner Construction announced the commencement of vertical construction for the $900 million expansion of the Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania. The 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower will increase the hospital's capacity to 500 beds and is expected to open in the first quarter of 2028, supporting up to 3,800 construction jobs and generating an estimated $1 billion in local economic impact.

Gilbane Development continues to expand its leadership and strategic partnerships to drive future growth. On April 28, 2026, the firm appointed Andreas Vlahakis as the new head of capital markets, based in New York City. Reporting to President and CEO James Patchett, Vlahakis will direct the firm's funding strategy and lead equity-raising efforts for ground-up multifamily and student housing investments. Meanwhile, Gilbane is moving forward with a major public-private partnership at Western Kentucky University. Following approval by the university's Board of Regents in December 2025 and authorization from the Kentucky Legislature on April 6, 2026, Gilbane is now cleared to design and develop new residence halls to replace outdated campus housing, signaling a continuous push toward modernizing student facilities.

value.Title: Report: Pennsylvania 's RGGI experiment cost billions in energy investment, stalled power projects

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-VTG3-RSC7-G2Y1-00000-00Report: Pennsylvania's RGGI experiment cost billions in energy investment, stalled power projects2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:08:35ZLexisNexisReport: Pennsylvania's RGGI experiment cost billions in energy investment, stalled power projects

The views expressed in any and all content distributed by Newstex and its re-distributors (collectively, the "Newstex Authoritative Content") are solely those of the respective author(s) and not necessarily the views of Newstex or its re-distributors. Stories from such authors are provided "AS IS," with no warranties, and confer no rights. The material and information provided in Newstex Authoritative Content are for general information only and should not, in any respect, be relied on as professional advice. Newstex Authoritative Content is not "read and approved" before it is posted. Accordingly, neither Newstex nor its re-distributors make any claims, promises or guarantees about the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of the information contained therein or linked to from such content, nor do they take responsibility for any aspect of such content. The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Bill Yingling

May 5th, 2026 ( Pennsylvania Business Report - Delivered by Newstex )

Pennsylvania's turbulent foray into the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative reduced investment in the state's electricity generation, resulting in a loss of energy projects and billions of dollars in revenue, according to a new analysis from the Commonwealth Foundation.

'The Keystone State's six-year on-again, off-again relationship with RGGI is a cautionary tale for other states, such as Virginia. RGGI is a tax on every user of electricity, toppling the energy future of every state it touches,' said Elizabeth Stelle, vice president of policy for the Commonwealth Foundation.

'In the six years under RGGI, Pennsylvania lost between $5 billion and $8 billion in investment in our energy sector,' she said in a written statement on May 4. 'The commonwealth lost enough electricity to power the entire Pittsburgh metro area.'

The Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative, or RGGI, is a multi-state cap-and-trade program among eastern states that sets regional limits on carbon dioxide emissions, requiring fossil fuel power producers to buy at auction allowances to cover their emissions.

Emissions targets are reduced over time, with the idea that the program gives power producers a financial incentive to invest in cleaner technology. Revenue from RGGI is intended to be used by states for efforts such as renewable power and energy efficiency.

Six years after attempting to enter the RGGI, Pennsylvania withdrew late last year after the courts ruled that the program represented an unconstitutional tax on producers and legislators voted to end the state's participation.

The issue of power plant development is significanttodayas increasingly tight power supplies are being blamed for increasing consumer electricity rates and anticipated use by data centers is forecast to drive up power demand even further while growth in supply struggles to keep pace.

The Commonwealth Foundation report said during Pennsylvania's effort, developers who had proposed billions of dollars in new power generation capacity during the Marcellus Shale natural gas boom stopped developing new projects, while few of the existing proposals reached operation.

The new report authored by Joshua Schubert, Energy Policy Analyst at the Commonwealth Foundation, says during 'Pennsylvania's RGGI Odyssey,' new generation proposals fell 38 percent and projects powered by natural gas 'collapsed by 65 percent.'

Natural gas is a considerably cleaner-burning fuel than coal or oil, and Pennsylvania has huge reserves.

At the same time, the list of projects in neighboring Ohio - which is not a participant in RGGI - increased.

'While Pennsylvania debated, litigated, and delayed over carbon tax policy, Ohio provided developers withapredictable regulatory environment to commit billions in capital,' Stelle said. 'Projects in Pennsylvania were cancelled as the project pipeline in Ohio boomed.'

Pennsylvania attempted to enter RGGI in 2019 with an executive order from Gov. Tom Wolf. Legislative Republicans and the industry fought the effort in court, saying it represented an unconstitutional tax.

The state's involvement ended in November 2025 when the legislature adopted a budget that extricated the state from the program. The Pennsylvania Supreme Court then in January dismissed the remaining appeals in the matter.

Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro advocates a state-level program to replace RGGI. The Commonwealth Foundation report, however, argues that it would cost Pennsylvania $157.2 billion and double residential electricity bills.

While Pennsylvania is leaving RGGI, Virginia is re-entering the program, a move that leaves the Commonwealth Foundation perplexed given the rising demand in northern Virginia.

'Pennsylvania's investment drought under RGGI should be a warning to other states as electricity demand surges,' Stelle said. 'Virginia's re-entry to the program under newly elected Gov. Abigail Spanberger boggles the mind as electricity demand in data center row soars.'

The post Report: Pennsylvania's RGGI experiment cost billions in energy investment, stalled power projects appeared first on Pennsylvania Business Report.

value.Title: Reed Smith boosts real estate finance, C-PACE capabilities with addition of partner Amanda A. Rapp

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHH-TKH3-RTWC-N1F4-00000-00Reed Smith boosts real estate finance, C-PACE capabilities with addition of partner Amanda A. Rapp2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisReed Smith boosts real estate finance, C-PACE capabilities with addition of partner Amanda A. RappTargeted News ServicePITTSBURGH, Pennsylvania

[Category: BizLaw/Legal] -- Reed Smith, a law firm, posted the following news release:

* * *

Reed Smith boosts real estate finance, C-PACE capabilities with addition of partner Amanda A. Rapp

*

WASHINGTON, D.C. - Global law firm, Reed Smith, announced the addition of Amanda A. Rapp as a partner in the firm's Financial Industry Group, affiliated with its Washington, D.C., office.

Rapp focuses her practice on commercial real estate financing, with particular depth in Commercial Property Assessed Clean Energy (C-PACE) financing and tax credit transactions.

"Amanda is a smart, driven attorney and a tremendous addition to the firm. Her ability to navigate the financing, real estate, and corporate dimensions of transactions is invaluable to both our team and our clients," said Keith M. Aurzada, global co-chair of Reed Smith's Financial Industry Group. "We have a top-tier real estate finance practice, and Amanda will further strengthen our capabilities, especially in C-PACE financing."

Rapp advises capital providers and property owners on C-PACE transactions ranging from straightforward capital stack enhancements to highly structured financings where C-PACE serves as the primary funding source. She guides clients through every stage of the deal lifecycle, from initial structuring to closing, and provides ongoing support in servicing and asset management. She also counsels clients on structured finance solutions for C-PACE assets, including securitizations and warehouse facilities. Amanda has closed a record number of C-PACE transactions across nearly every active jurisdiction, reflecting the product's evolution from a niche solution to a widely adopted financing tool.

In addition, Rapp brings broad experience in tax credit financing, advising lenders, investors, and sponsors on transactions involving new markets tax credits, historic rehabilitation tax credits, and renewable energy tax credits. She also represents clients in the development, acquisition, and financing (including C-PACE financing) of data centers.

"Reed Smith's real estate finance team has earned a reputation for building lasting client relationships by anticipating market trends and delivering results," said Rapp. "I'm excited to join a group operating at such a high level and to contribute my experience in C-PACE and tax credit financing."

Rapp joins from Stinson LLP. She earned her J.D., cum laude, from Saint Louis University School of Law and her B.A., magna cum laude, from Saint Louis University.

About Reed Smith

Reed Smith is a dynamic international law firm dedicated to helping clients move their businesses forward. With an inclusive culture and innovative mindset, we deliver smarter, more creative legal services that drive better outcomes for our clients. Our deep industry knowledge, long-standing relationships and collaborative structure make us the go-to partner for complex disputes, transactions, and regulatory matters.

***

urn:contentItem:6JK8-2JG3-SBS5-021J-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MAINTAINS CASH FLOW MOMENTUM AS DIVIDEND RISES 5.3% TO $0.20 PER SHARE

Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) posted adjusted EPS of 0.31 USD and free cash flow of approximately 1.1 billion USD for the quarter, enabling a 5.3% dividend increase to 0.20 USD per share. The core business comprises roughly 80,000 miles of pipelines and 140 terminals transporting natural gas, crude oil, refined products and chemicals. Natural gas pipelines drive revenue, with demand from power generation and industrial users; the Trans-Canada Keystone pipeline system serves Permian and Gulf Coast production areas. The company also operates CO2 pipelines and enhanced oil recovery, along with terminals and product pipelines that sustain recurring cash flow to fund the dividend and selective growth initiatives. These assets support ongoing cash flow and provide a foundation for future growth, while investors weigh regulatory and demand-related risks as energy policy evolves

+ KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) ADVANCES TRIDENT PIPELINE AND MONUMENT ACQUISITION TO CAPTURE LNG UPSIDE

On 5th May 2026, Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) discussed how geopolitical tensions are lifting demand for U.S. LNG and pipeline capacity. President Dax Sanders noted that U.S. producers remain disciplined while watching long-term price signals that could prompt higher output, with energy security concerns supporting additional Permian Basin volumes.Sanders pointed to geopolitics boosting demand for LNG and pipeline capacity. He cited a reduction of about 2-3 Bcf/d in Middle Eastern liquefaction capacity and industry forecasts suggesting growth of around 19 Bcf/d in the U.S. gas market over the next four to five years, much of it from LNG. Kinder Morgan moves roughly 40% of U.S. gas and liquefaction feed.To capture anticipated demand, Kinder Morgan Posts a $10.1 billion sanctioned backlog of projects, including the under-construction Trident Pipeline with initial 2 Bcf/d capacity and potential expansion. The Monument Pipeline was acquired for about $500 million, strengthening its regional footprint.

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JK3-R6T3-RSHC-73K0-00000-00Alcoa Corporation (NYSE: AA, ASX: AAI) Reports $65 Million Investment to Expand Mosjøen Foundry in Norway2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:42ZLexisNexisAlcoa Corporation (NYSE: AA, ASX: AAI) Reports $65 Million Investment to Expand Mosjøen Foundry in Norway

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

13 May 2026 10:15 EDT

Alcoa Corporation (NYSE: AA, ASX: AAI) is investing $65 million to expand its foundry production at the Mosjøen smelter in Norway, a move that aligns with the companys plan to deliver low carbon aluminum products to customers in key markets and to begin incorporating recycled content into the casting process for the first time since the 12th May 2026 announcement. The plan follows a continued effort to grow sustainable capabilities in Europe.
