urn:contentItem:6JHS-XX63-RTHK-1270-00000-00WEEKLY CORPORATE REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisWEEKLY CORPORATE REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. [4 Press Releases]

+ PETER LANDAU JOINS LAW FIRM BABST CALLAND

Peter J. Landau recently joined law firm Babst Calland's Pittsburgh, Pa. office as an associate in the Corporate and Commercial and Emerging Technologies groups. He advises clients across a variety of industries on a broad range of corporate and commercial legal matters, including mergers and acquisitions, commercial contracts, corporate structuring and governance, and general corporate and business counseling.

Before joining the Firm, Mr. Landau was an associate with DLA Piper in Palo Alto, Calif., where he advised U.S.- and foreign-based companies on domestic and international tax matters, including international, M&A, state and local, and renewable energy tax credits. He is a 2022 graduate of Columbia Law School.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. ANNA SKIPPER JEWART SELECTED BY THE LEGAL INTELLIGENCER AS A "2026 LAWYER ON THE FAST TRACK, SMALL/MIDSIZE FIRMS WINNER"

Babst Calland Attorney Anna Skipper Jewart was selected by The Legal Intelligencer as a "2026 Lawyer on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winner" in Pennsylvania.

After reviewing the results of nominations for the annual The Legal Intelligencer awards, a judging panel composed of evaluators from all corners of the legal profession and the state selected 11 attorneys as the 2026 Lawyers on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winners. This recognition is only given to attorneys under the age of 40 who have demonstrated excellence in four categories: development of the law; advocacy and community contributions; service to the bar; and peer and public recognition.

Ms. Jewart is an associate in the Firm's Public Sector, Real Estate, Land Use and Zoning, Energy and Natural Resources, and Data Center Development groups. Her practice focuses on municipal and land use law in both the private and public sectors. Ms. Jewart assists private clients in navigating zoning and other land use processes across Pennsylvania. In particular, she specializes in renewable energy development, data center development, and similar industries. In addition, Ms. Jewart represents public clients in municipal solicitorship and special counsel roles. She is a 2019 graduate, cum laude, Boston College of Law.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. DON BLUEDORN AND ETHAN JOHNSON DISCUSS GENAI PRODUCTS FOR USE IN LEGAL PRACTICE AT ACC DRAFT-ING DAY

Attorneys Don Bluedorn and Ethan Johnson presented "The AI Playbook - Strategizing Artificial Intelligence in Legal Practice" at the Western Pennsylvania Chapter of the Association of Corporate Counsel's (ACC) Draft-ing Day.

Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping the legal profession, much like how NFL teams draft, strategize, and execute on the field. This CLE program provided attorneys and in-house counsel with an "AI playbook" designed to help understand, implement, and manage artificial intelligence technologies while maintaining compliance, ethical integrity, and risk mitigation.

Don and Ethan discussed GenAI products for use in the legal practice, focusing on available products, how the products can be most effectively used in the legal practice, and best practices to avoid GenAI-related pitfalls (both for lawyers using GenAI and for lawyers supervising others who are using GenAI).

Source: Company Website

+ THERESA DAVIS JOINS BABST CALLAND'S HARRISBURG OFFICE

Theresa Davis recently joined Babst Calland's Harrisburg, Pa. office as an associate in the Employment and Labor and Public Sector groups. Ms. Davis represents public and private employers in a wide range of labor and employment matters. Her practice includes all aspects of state and federal litigation involving employment claims, as well as advising clients on day-to-day workplace issues and compliance strategies. She also represents municipal clients on a wide range of matters.

Source: Company Website

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHS-S813-S8K8-R2R8-00000-00May 07, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO PEER GROUP UPDATE: KEY RECENT NEWS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSMay 07, 2026: CONSIGLI CONSTRUCTION CO PEER GROUP UPDATE: KEY RECENT NEWS

On February 25, 2026, Consigli Construction Co. commenced work on the Williams College Museum of Art, marking the institution's first purpose-built facility on campus. Abigail Berkebile, a figure at Consigli Construction, highlighted the complexity and unique design of the project, which aims to enhance student access to art collections. Berkebile also utilized the announcement to emphasize the diverse opportunities available for women in the construction industry, noting that project teams rely on broad expertise spanning accounting, legal matters, IT, and procurement, allowing professionals to advance without a traditional field background.

In peer news, Suffolk Construction continues to manage the Southeastern Public Service Authority interchange project, which facilitated the reopening of a long-term westbound lane closure on U.S. Route 58 in Virginia on May 5, 2026. The project is designed to build a new ramp and flyover for eastbound traffic to improve safety and regional efficiency. Separately, on May 1, 2026, Virgin Media O2 announced a 10-year Power Purchase Agreement to source renewable energy from a 70-megawatt solar farm currently under construction in Suffolk. The solar farm is anticipated to commence power generation in 2027 and will supply approximately 5 percent of the telecommunications company's total energy demands as it targets Net Zero carbon emissions by 2040.

Turner Construction Company achieved a significant milestone on April 28, 2026, with the topping out of the 450,000-square-foot, 72-megawatt Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland. Nick Sevier, Operations Manager at Turner Construction Company, stated, "Topping out this facility represents more than completing the structural frame. It reflects the strength of our partnership with Aligned, the dedication of our trade partners, and Turner's commitment to delivering this complex, high-performance data center safely and efficiently." On the same day, Turner Construction announced the start of vertical construction on the $900 million expansion of the Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center in Pennsylvania. The project features an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will increase capacity to 500 beds by the first quarter of 2028. Additionally, on April 22, 2026, Turner was named the general contractor for the $500 million Westcourt Orlando mixed-use development, which will feature a hotel, residential units, and an entertainment venue.

Gilbane Development announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets on April 28, 2026. Operating out of the New York City office, Vlahakis is tasked with refining the firm's funding strategy and leading transaction structuring. In other company developments, Gilbane launched leasing for Phase II of its $135 million affordable housing redevelopment at 7th Street and Barnaby Road in Washington, D.C., on April 3, 2026. Slated for completion by summer 2026, the second phase comprises 229 units across four buildings, with twenty percent of the apartments designated as Permanent Supportive Housing managed by the district's Department of Homeless Services.

urn:contentItem:6JHR-KV33-S2KX-K4T9-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Alcoa reports 7.4% sequential fall in Quarterly Revenue2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Alcoa reports 7.4% sequential fall in Quarterly Revenue

AMERICAN RESULTS

Alcoa (NYSE:AA), announced total revenue of $3.2b for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], down 7.4% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and down 5.2% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit slumped 22.4% from $548.0m to $425.0m

- Total revenue was down 5.2% from $3.4b to $3.2b

- EPS slumped 22.7% from $2.07 to $1.6

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 19.8% to 15.6%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 19.8% to 15.6%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 16.3% to 13.3%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Revenues was down from 3.6% to 2.6%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 22.7%, Revenue was down 5.2% and Net Profit was down 22.4%.

ACTIVITIES:

Alcoa Corporation is a leading global producer of bauxite, alumina, and aluminum, headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company operates across the full aluminum value chain, encompassing bauxite mining, alumina refining, and aluminum smelting and casting, serving diverse industries such as aerospace, automotive, packaging, and construction. Alcoa's core activities emphasize sustainable production practices, including low-carbon aluminum manufacturing to meet environmental standards. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been its aluminum products for electric vehicles and renewable energy infrastructure, driven by rising demand for lightweight, eco-friendly materials that enhance energy efficiency. This strategic focus has positioned Alcoa to capitalize on global trends toward decarbonization and electrification, while maintaining operational excellence and innovation in its core processes.

MARKET POSITION:

Alcoa Corp (NYSE:AA), the NYSE's 3rd largest Basic Materials company by market cap, jumped $17.28 (or 32.6%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $70.32. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 2.9% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 29.7%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $2,011 [vs $1,397 for the S&P 500 Index], for a capital gain of $967 and dividend reinvested of $45. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 41.8% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 5 million shares so far in 2026 was 0.9 times the average daily volume in 2025.

urn:contentItem:6JHR-F9P3-SCRX-638S-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - COMPREHENSIVE NEWS FROM INDUSTRY AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

+ OPEN SEASON SUCCESS SPURS NEXT PHASE FOR WESTERN GATEWAY REFINED-PRODUCTS CORRIDOR

Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan announced that the initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline attracted strong market interest and solid shipper commitments, reinforcing demand for added refined-products transportation capacity into the Southwest and California. The partners said the response warrants moving forward with a second open season in January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and to introduce expanded delivery options. The next period will bring new destination points west of Colton, California, made available through a joint tariff with an SFPP, L.P. segment that will be reconfigured to support westbound flows between Watson and Colton, ensuring shippers can reach the Los Angeles market more directly.The Western Gateway project comprises a new-build segment from Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona and the integration of Kinder Morgan's existing SFPP, L.P. pipeline from Colton to Phoenix. As part of the plan, the SFPP, L.P. segment will be reversed to move refined products from east to west into California, aligning with the project’s objective to shift flows toward West Coast demand centers. The system will draw supply from Borger and El Paso, Texas, connecting midcontinent refining with Southwest demand centers, and will include reversing the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to route midcontinent products into Western Gateway.Once complete, the initiative is designed to create a robust transport corridor linking Midwest refining with growth markets in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas, via the CALNEV extension. The project seeks to diversify supply, improve energy security, and support sustained demand growth across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: WEEKLY REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-YGX3-RTWH-J4YM-00000-00WEEKLY REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisWEEKLY REVIEW: BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. NEWS THIS PAST WEEK

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. [4 Press Releases]

+ PETER LANDAU JOINS LAW FIRM BABST CALLAND

Peter J. Landau recently joined law firm Babst Calland's Pittsburgh, Pa. office as an associate in the Corporate and Commercial and Emerging Technologies groups. He advises clients across a variety of industries on a broad range of corporate and commercial legal matters, including mergers and acquisitions, commercial contracts, corporate structuring and governance, and general corporate and business counseling.

Before joining the Firm, Mr. Landau was an associate with DLA Piper in Palo Alto, Calif., where he advised U.S.- and foreign-based companies on domestic and international tax matters, including international, M&A, state and local, and renewable energy tax credits. He is a 2022 graduate of Columbia Law School.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. ANNA SKIPPER JEWART SELECTED BY THE LEGAL INTELLIGENCER AS A "2026 LAWYER ON THE FAST TRACK, SMALL/MIDSIZE FIRMS WINNER"

Babst Calland Attorney Anna Skipper Jewart was selected by The Legal Intelligencer as a "2026 Lawyer on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winner" in Pennsylvania.

After reviewing the results of nominations for the annual The Legal Intelligencer awards, a judging panel composed of evaluators from all corners of the legal profession and the state selected 11 attorneys as the 2026 Lawyers on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winners. This recognition is only given to attorneys under the age of 40 who have demonstrated excellence in four categories: development of the law; advocacy and community contributions; service to the bar; and peer and public recognition.

Ms. Jewart is an associate in the Firm's Public Sector, Real Estate, Land Use and Zoning, Energy and Natural Resources, and Data Center Development groups. Her practice focuses on municipal and land use law in both the private and public sectors. Ms. Jewart assists private clients in navigating zoning and other land use processes across Pennsylvania. In particular, she specializes in renewable energy development, data center development, and similar industries. In addition, Ms. Jewart represents public clients in municipal solicitorship and special counsel roles. She is a 2019 graduate, cum laude, Boston College of Law.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. DON BLUEDORN AND ETHAN JOHNSON DISCUSS GENAI PRODUCTS FOR USE IN LEGAL PRACTICE AT ACC DRAFT-ING DAY

Attorneys Don Bluedorn and Ethan Johnson presented "The AI Playbook - Strategizing Artificial Intelligence in Legal Practice" at the Western Pennsylvania Chapter of the Association of Corporate Counsel's (ACC) Draft-ing Day.

Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping the legal profession, much like how NFL teams draft, strategize, and execute on the field. This CLE program provided attorneys and in-house counsel with an "AI playbook" designed to help understand, implement, and manage artificial intelligence technologies while maintaining compliance, ethical integrity, and risk mitigation.

Don and Ethan discussed GenAI products for use in the legal practice, focusing on available products, how the products can be most effectively used in the legal practice, and best practices to avoid GenAI-related pitfalls (both for lawyers using GenAI and for lawyers supervising others who are using GenAI).

Source: Company Website

+ THERESA DAVIS JOINS BABST CALLAND'S HARRISBURG OFFICE

Theresa Davis recently joined Babst Calland's Harrisburg, Pa. office as an associate in the Employment and Labor and Public Sector groups. Ms. Davis represents public and private employers in a wide range of labor and employment matters. Her practice includes all aspects of state and federal litigation involving employment claims, as well as advising clients on day-to-day workplace issues and compliance strategies. She also represents municipal clients on a wide range of matters.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHK-Y103-S8YC-S300-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Alcoa reports 88% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 17 April 2026 07:45 EDT Release Date: April 16, 20262026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Alcoa reports 88% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 17 April 2026 07:45 EDT Release Date: April 16, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Alcoa (NYSE:AA), announced net profit of $425m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 88% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and down 22% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 86% sequentially from 86.0c in Q4/2025 to $1.6 in Q1/2026.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Unfavourable Changes:

- Net profit slumped 22.4% from $548.0m to $425.0m

- Total revenue was down 5.2% from $3.4b to $3.2b

- EPS slumped 22.7% from $2.07 to $1.6

- EBIT to Revenues was down from 19.8% to 15.6%

- Profit before tax to Revenues was down from 19.8% to 15.6%

- Profit after tax to Revenues was down from 16.3% to 13.3%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Revenues was down from 3.6% to 2.6%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was down 22.7%, Revenue was down 5.2% and Net Profit was down 22.4%.

ACTIVITIES:

Alcoa Corporation is a leading global producer of bauxite, alumina, and aluminum, headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company operates across the full aluminum value chain, encompassing bauxite mining, alumina refining, and aluminum smelting and casting, serving diverse industries such as aerospace, automotive, packaging, and construction. Alcoa's core activities emphasize sustainable production practices, including low-carbon aluminum manufacturing to meet environmental standards. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been its aluminum products for electric vehicles and renewable energy infrastructure, driven by rising demand for lightweight, eco-friendly materials that enhance energy efficiency. This strategic focus has positioned Alcoa to capitalize on global trends toward decarbonization and electrification, while maintaining operational excellence and innovation in its core processes.

MARKET POSITION:

Alcoa Corp (NYSE:AA), the NYSE's 3rd largest Basic Materials company by market cap, jumped $17.28 (or 32.6%) year-to-date (YTD) in 2026 to close at $70.32. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 2.9% YTD, this is a relative price increase of 29.7%. A zero-bagger in 2025, the value of USD1,000 invested two years ago is $2,011 [vs $1,397 for the S&P 500 Index], for a capital gain of $967 and dividend reinvested of $45. The average annualised return to shareholders has been 41.8% in the past two years. The average daily volume of 5 million shares so far in 2026 was 0.9 times the average daily volume in 2025.

value.Title: COLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-92P3-RWC8-P4GB-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

+ WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE GAINS MOMENTUM, LINKING MIDWEST REFINERIES TO WESTERN MARKETS

The initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline has closed with strong market interest, and Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan say the response supports advancing the project. A second open season is planned for January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and expanded delivery options, including new destinations west of Colton through a joint tariff with SFPP, L.P. that reconfigures the Colton-Phoenix segment to support westbound flows.The Western Gateway includes a new Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona line and the reversal of the SFPP, L.P. Colton-Phoenix segment. The plan also reverses the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to feed midcontinent refineries and supply Western Gateway’s western deliveries.The project aims to connect Midwest refining capacity with growing demand in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing supply diversity and energy security across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHH-G7C3-SFSX-M17S-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - THE RECENT ON INDUSTRY AND PEER DEVELOPMENTS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

+ OPEN SEASON SUCCESS SPURS NEXT PHASE FOR WESTERN GATEWAY REFINED-PRODUCTS CORRIDOR

Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan announced that the initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline attracted strong market interest and solid shipper commitments, reinforcing demand for added refined-products transportation capacity into the Southwest and California. The partners said the response warrants moving forward with a second open season in January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and to introduce expanded delivery options. The next period will bring new destination points west of Colton, California, made available through a joint tariff with an SFPP, L.P. segment that will be reconfigured to support westbound flows between Watson and Colton, ensuring shippers can reach the Los Angeles market more directly.The Western Gateway project comprises a new-build segment from Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona and the integration of Kinder Morgan's existing SFPP, L.P. pipeline from Colton to Phoenix. As part of the plan, the SFPP, L.P. segment will be reversed to move refined products from east to west into California, aligning with the project’s objective to shift flows toward West Coast demand centers. The system will draw supply from Borger and El Paso, Texas, connecting midcontinent refining with Southwest demand centers, and will include reversing the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to route midcontinent products into Western Gateway.Once complete, the initiative is designed to create a robust transport corridor linking Midwest refining with growth markets in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas, via the CALNEV extension. The project seeks to diversify supply, improve energy security, and support sustained demand growth across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: In Albany , GOP candidate for governor lays out ‘all of the above’ energy policy
value.Overview: Watertown Daily Times (New York), BUSINESS AND FINANCIAL NEWS, 1335words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-K9V3-RV85-Y1SD-00000-00In Albany, GOP candidate for governor lays out ‘all of the above’ energy policy2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:04:53ZLexisNexisBUSINESS AND FINANCIAL NEWSIn Albany, GOP candidate for governor lays out ‘all of the above’ energy policyAlex GaultWatertown Daily Times, N.Y.

ALBANY — Energy prices across New York are climbing and currently sit almost 70% higher than the national average. Bruce R. Blakeman, the Republican candidate for governor this year, has a plan that he says will change that.

Standing alongside most of the state Senate and Assembly Republican conferences, Blakeman and the state’s GOP leaders blasted Democrats, and Gov. Kathleen C. Hochul, for backing policies they say are driving up the costs of energy in New York, especially efforts to electrify more buildings across the state and decarbonize the energy grid.

“When you examine (your energy bill) and look at it, only 30% of your payment actually goes to energy,” he said. “The rest goes to excessive delivery fees, more taxes because they tax your utility bills, and the green energy scam. It is a scam.”

Blakeman blasted the renewable energy facilities that have gone up across upstate New York; solar facilities he says are covered in snow most of the winter, offshore wind farms he says are among the most expensive per-megawatt of generated power in the world, as well as the state’s now decades-long block on extracting natural gas from the Marcellus Shale in the Southern Tier.

“Kathy Hochul is hell bent on making us all-electric, when we know it’s a pipe dream, we know it can’t happen,” he said.

Blakeman supports ending the moratorium on fracking and other methods of natural gas extraction; he argued that the boom of natural gas wells in northern Pennsylvania on that state’s share of the Marcellus Shale have driven an economic boom in northern Pennsylvania that could be felt in the Southern Tier too.

“When you drive along the Southern Tier of this state, if you go to the Pennsylvania side, people are happy, they’re prosperous,” he said. “They’ve got new roads, new buildings, they’re driving around in brand new Cadillac Escalades. You go on the other side of the border, in New York, it’s poverty, it’s despair, people are driving around in 25-year-old rusted out pickup trucks.”

The data doesn’t seem to back up Blakeman’s argument. While the counties in northern Pennsylvania that host natural gas wells did see a 50% bump in their GDP between 2008 and 2020, the region actually lost over 3% of its jobs in the same time period. And natural gas is increasing in price, and is expected to only get more expensive; in Pennsylvania, the state’s reliance on natural gas to heat homes and run power plants means that those increasing prices are putting pressure on resident’s energy bills. And as the infrastructure to move more of that Pennsylvanian gas to other markets was built up, more and more of it was sent to markets willing to pay higher prices for it. The Ohio River Valley Institute found that about 90% of the revenue from the industry left the state.

“After more than a decade of immense investment, natural gas development has shown itself to be an ineffective vehicle for economic development at best and at worst it may be destructive to job creation and population growth in the region,” said Sean O’Leary, a senior researcher with the Ohio River Valley Institute who authored their report on the industry’s impact in Pennsylvania. “The data are quite clear that natural gas expansion is not a foundation upon which job growth and economic development can be built.”

Blakeman said that affordability and energy costs go hand in hand, and are a major driver of New York’s population loss. He pointed to his own son who now lives in Florida.

“People are happy there,” he said. “You ride around in Florida, people have jobs, there’s prosperity, there’s building everywhere. You ride around this sate, people are complaining, they’re miserable. They want a governor that’s going to make them happy.”

His plan, if elected, would involve pushing to repeal the green energy mandates that are pushing the utility companies to spend billions to upgrade their power infrastructure, which is what’s been driving up delivery fees on power bills. He would allow for fracking in the Southern Tier, and he would order the state Energy Research and Development Authority to return the $2.4 billion it’s collected from ratepayer fees for green energy projects, which he argues will drive bills down by half.

Blakeman also wants to repeal the state law that is forcing new construction to go all-electric; he described it as an effort to dictate what stoves and equipment people can have in their homes, which he said will only drive up costs even further and force the food industry into using more expensive, worse quality equipment.

“Talk to restaurants, and people who have diners, ask how many of them are electric,” he said. “None! They’re going to have to replace that equipment, the cost of food is going to go up, and how is that going to make it more affordable?”

The state’s electrification laws don’t require retroactive replacement of existing equipment, but rather they mandate that new construction cannot include natural gas or fossil fuel connections.

When asked if he rejects the concept of man-made climate change, which is the reasoning behind much of the green energy policy that he opposes, Blakeman didn’t give a straight answer. He said he supports green energy policies that are backed by science and make sense from an economic standpoint.

“But to take drastic measures and pretend that you’re actually going to change the carbon footprint of our state, and that is going to have a material impact on the world is a complete fiction,” he said. “First of all, our carbon footprint is miniscule compared to the rest of the world here in New York state. And when you look at the cost-benefit analysis, you don’t get the returns from green energy.”

Blakeman says he supports an “all of the above” energy policy that relies on fossil fuels, next-generation nuclear power as well as responsible and proportional renewable energy facilities.

The Governor says the same thing, and the recently-completed state energy plan that her administration helped to pull together calls for reliance on all available forms of power generation into the future, and a phased approach to closing fossil fuel-driven plants.

When asked, Blakeman said their approaches are vastly different.

“Her ‘all of the above’ is electric, electric, electric, green energy, green energy, scam the people, scam the people, scam the people,” he said. “I’ve told you what mine is. She hasn’t changed her policies.”

Blakeman’s visit to Albany came as leaders were putting together the final pieces of the state’s annual budget, now well over a month overdue. Most of that negotiation has gone on behind closed doors with only small details leaked to the press as talks have continued on a variety of policy issues.

Even as talks were wrapping up, details on a push from Hochul to change parts of the state’s climate law, block law enforcement from working with federal immigration authorities, change state laws on auto insurance liability and change the rules around environmental reviews for housing projects are still not clear. And the final total for how much New York will spend this fiscal year was still unclear as well.

Blakeman pledged that if elected he would work to bring more transparency to the state budgeting process by driving negotiations through the constitutionally-mandated conference committees that are rarely used in modern budget talks. But he said that would likely only work if the Republicans managed to take control of the state Assembly and Senate too.

“It takes three to tango in this town,” he said, referencing the power of the Governor, Senate Majority Leader and Assembly Speaker. “But I am very confident that I will gave an on time budget, a budget that is a lot less than what will be proposed (this year), somewhere in the range of $270 billion, because I will have Majority Leader Rob Ortt and Speaker Ra, and they will be great partners with me in this process.”

value.Title: NextEra Energy Inc. May Consider Positioning Advantages Following Exelon Corporation 's Sall, Ballard, Blay, Broderick et al. v. PECO Energy Gas Incident Litigation in Utilities Sector *Updated*

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-T733-S007-71NS-00000-00NextEra Energy Inc. May Consider Positioning Advantages Following Exelon Corporation's Sall, Ballard, Blay, Broderick et al. v. PECO Energy Gas Incident Litigation in Utilities Sector *Updated*2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisNextEra Energy Inc. May Consider Positioning Advantages Following Exelon Corporation's Sall, Ballard, Blay, Broderick et al. v. PECO Energy Gas Incident Litigation in Utilities Sector *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

What Stakeholders Need To Know

The litigation concerning a natural gas explosion at a healthcare facility underscores vulnerabilities within gas distribution and emergency response frameworks in the utilities sector. Stakeholders are advised to track developments that might lead to more rigorous regulatory evaluations of aging infrastructure and accountability standards. The results could increase compliance expenses and affect how energy companies handle public safety requirements. This situation also indicates broader industry requirements to improve coordination with facility operators during gas leak incidents, which may alter risk assessment protocols and insurance structures. Competitive positioning may change as organizations assess their internal gas safety programs to mitigate related risks.

Background of the Case

The litigation, filed on or around January 4, 2026, centers on allegations of negligence against PECO Energy Company and Exelon Corporation regarding a December 23, 2025, natural gas explosion at the Bristol Health & Rehab Center in Bucks County, Pennsylvania. Initiated in the Philadelphia Court of Common Pleas (Case ID 260100101), the lawsuit seeks damages related to fatalities and injuries that occurred after reports of a gas odor were allegedly not addressed with remediation or evacuation. The filing includes additional defendants such as Bristol Health & Rehab Center LLC and Saber Healthcare entities. Discovery is currently in progress, supported by context from the National Transportation Safety Board investigation. The proceedings evaluate utility response procedures and facility management, which may impact Exelon's natural gas distribution operations.

Competitive Impacts on NextEra Energy, Inc.

NextEra Energy, Inc., which focuses on clean energy and regulated operations, may experience sector-wide effects necessitating a review of its gas-related assets and safety protocols. The proceedings could increase the industry's focus on integrated safety systems, which may affect NextEra's capital allocation and public reporting concerning operational reliability.

Possible Opportunities for NextEra Energy, Inc.

Highlight transition plans aimed at decreasing reliance on legacy gas infrastructure.

Create safety and resilience programs for critical infrastructure customers.

Address investor interest in proactive safety governance models.

Establish partnerships with regulatory bodies regarding updated response frameworks.

Why This Case Is Challenging for NextEra Energy, Inc.

Increased regulatory oversight could result in extra operational costs for existing gas assets. Legal precedents from this situation might elevate litigation risks in vulnerable regions. Public attention regarding utility accountability may affect permitting schedules for future expansion projects. Requirements for compliance and reputation management could utilize resources otherwise allocated for renewable energy development.

Key Challenges for NextEra Energy, Inc.

Industry-wide expense increases related to safety compliance.

Legal precedents that may impact standard operations.

Stakeholder requirements regarding incident prevention strategies.

Managing safety expenditures alongside clean energy objectives.

Competitor's Strategic Responses

NextEra Energy, Inc. may execute specific initiatives to manage these industry changes.

Review and update safety features for applicable gas assets.

Incorporate advanced analytics within emergency preparedness protocols.

Provide stakeholders with objective safety performance metrics.

Coordinate public communications to reflect resilient infrastructure practices.

Key Measures: Implementation would involve cross-functional teams, specific capital projects, and continuous regulatory discussions to maintain compliance and support NextEra's market position regarding safety.

Possible Losses and Unexpected Advantages

Potential Advantages

Increased investor interest resulting from documented safety commitments.

Avenues to establish hybrid energy safety standards.

Improved interactions with regulatory agencies focused on safety.

Additional business from customers emphasizing reliability.

Potential Losses

Increased costs for system upgrades that could reach substantial amounts.

Extended project schedules due to heightened regulatory review.

Financial impacts resulting from utility sector insurance adjustments.

Near-term perception issues affecting utility industry participants.

Final Thoughts

NextEra Energy, Inc. has the capacity to address the conditions presented by this litigation by emphasizing its operational resilience and responsibility. Strategic initiatives prioritizing technology, transparency, and collaboration will assist the organization in managing risks and finding structural advantages within the utilities sector.

Tags: Sall, Ballard, Blay, Broderick et al. v. PECO Energy Co., Exelon Corp., Bristol Health and Rehab Center LLC, Saber Healthcare Holdings et al.

Activities

Exelon Corporation is a prominent U.S.-based energy company listed on the Nasdaq, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity, as well as natural gas delivery. As one of the largest utilities in the nation, it operates through subsidiaries like Commonwealth Edison, PECO, and BGE, serving millions of customers across multiple states. The company's core activities encompass a diverse energy portfolio, including nuclear, fossil fuel, and renewable sources, with a strong emphasis on reliable and sustainable power supply. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, driven by increasing demand for clean energy and supportive regulatory environments. Exelon is also advancing grid modernization and smart technology investments to enhance efficiency and integrate more renewables, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon future while maintaining operational excellence and financial stability. It is the NASDAQ's 4th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Exelon Corporation, NASDAQ:EXC>

Exelon Corporation's capex plan is strategically crafted, encompassing the EXC capex guidance and a forward-looking Exelon capital expenditure plan. The Exelon 10-year capex plan is an integral part of its capital deployment strategy, emphasizing Exelon rate base growth and infrastructure investment. A focus on Exelon grid modernization projects and smart grid upgrades marks a significant stride in enhancing energy delivery. Key projects include Exelon transmission projects and distribution upgrades, underlining a strong commitment to regulated utility capex. The Exelon nuclear fleet investment along with renewable energy investments affirms the company’s dedication to a sustainable future. Notably, Exelon's battery storage projects further reinforce this commitment, supported by clean energy transition capex. Maintenance and growth capex are pivotal as the capex targets evolve. The company’s capex strategy is outlined in the Exelon investor presentation, impacting both earnings and dividend outlook. As the Exelon stock EXC garners attention, its target price reflects dynamic shifts like rate case impact and capital allocation priorities. The inclusion of Exelon ESG and decarbonization targets highlights its aligned infrastructure spending forecast. Evidently, the story is shifting for Exelon, with capital spending cresting towards 2026.

urn:contentItem:6JHB-9XG3-RSRS-S4Y7-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

+ WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE GAINS MOMENTUM, LINKING MIDWEST REFINERIES TO WESTERN MARKETS

The initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline has closed with strong market interest, and Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan say the response supports advancing the project. A second open season is planned for January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and expanded delivery options, including new destinations west of Colton through a joint tariff with SFPP, L.P. that reconfigures the Colton-Phoenix segment to support westbound flows.The Western Gateway includes a new Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona line and the reversal of the SFPP, L.P. Colton-Phoenix segment. The plan also reverses the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to feed midcontinent refineries and supply Western Gateway’s western deliveries.The project aims to connect Midwest refining capacity with growing demand in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing supply diversity and energy security across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. [4 Press Releases]

+ PETER LANDAU JOINS LAW FIRM BABST CALLAND

Peter J. Landau recently joined law firm Babst Calland's Pittsburgh, Pa. office as an associate in the Corporate and Commercial and Emerging Technologies groups. He advises clients across a variety of industries on a broad range of corporate and commercial legal matters, including mergers and acquisitions, commercial contracts, corporate structuring and governance, and general corporate and business counseling.

Before joining the Firm, Mr. Landau was an associate with DLA Piper in Palo Alto, Calif., where he advised U.S.- and foreign-based companies on domestic and international tax matters, including international, M&A, state and local, and renewable energy tax credits. He is a 2022 graduate of Columbia Law School.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. ANNA SKIPPER JEWART SELECTED BY THE LEGAL INTELLIGENCER AS A "2026 LAWYER ON THE FAST TRACK, SMALL/MIDSIZE FIRMS WINNER"

Babst Calland Attorney Anna Skipper Jewart was selected by The Legal Intelligencer as a "2026 Lawyer on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winner" in Pennsylvania.

After reviewing the results of nominations for the annual The Legal Intelligencer awards, a judging panel composed of evaluators from all corners of the legal profession and the state selected 11 attorneys as the 2026 Lawyers on the Fast Track, Small/Midsize Firms Winners. This recognition is only given to attorneys under the age of 40 who have demonstrated excellence in four categories: development of the law; advocacy and community contributions; service to the bar; and peer and public recognition.

Ms. Jewart is an associate in the Firm's Public Sector, Real Estate, Land Use and Zoning, Energy and Natural Resources, and Data Center Development groups. Her practice focuses on municipal and land use law in both the private and public sectors. Ms. Jewart assists private clients in navigating zoning and other land use processes across Pennsylvania. In particular, she specializes in renewable energy development, data center development, and similar industries. In addition, Ms. Jewart represents public clients in municipal solicitorship and special counsel roles. She is a 2019 graduate, cum laude, Boston College of Law.

Source: Company Website

+ BABST CALLAND CLEMENTS AND ZOMNIR P.C. DON BLUEDORN AND ETHAN JOHNSON DISCUSS GENAI PRODUCTS FOR USE IN LEGAL PRACTICE AT ACC DRAFT-ING DAY

Attorneys Don Bluedorn and Ethan Johnson presented "The AI Playbook - Strategizing Artificial Intelligence in Legal Practice" at the Western Pennsylvania Chapter of the Association of Corporate Counsel's (ACC) Draft-ing Day.

Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping the legal profession, much like how NFL teams draft, strategize, and execute on the field. This CLE program provided attorneys and in-house counsel with an "AI playbook" designed to help understand, implement, and manage artificial intelligence technologies while maintaining compliance, ethical integrity, and risk mitigation.

Don and Ethan discussed GenAI products for use in the legal practice, focusing on available products, how the products can be most effectively used in the legal practice, and best practices to avoid GenAI-related pitfalls (both for lawyers using GenAI and for lawyers supervising others who are using GenAI).

Source: Company Website

+ THERESA DAVIS JOINS BABST CALLAND'S HARRISBURG OFFICE

Theresa Davis recently joined Babst Calland's Harrisburg, Pa. office as an associate in the Employment and Labor and Public Sector groups. Ms. Davis represents public and private employers in a wide range of labor and employment matters. Her practice includes all aspects of state and federal litigation involving employment claims, as well as advising clients on day-to-day workplace issues and compliance strategies. She also represents municipal clients on a wide range of matters.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JH9-GXP3-S940-Y346-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT NEWS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT NEWS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

+ OPEN SEASON SUCCESS SPURS NEXT PHASE FOR WESTERN GATEWAY REFINED-PRODUCTS CORRIDOR

Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan announced that the initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline attracted strong market interest and solid shipper commitments, reinforcing demand for added refined-products transportation capacity into the Southwest and California. The partners said the response warrants moving forward with a second open season in January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and to introduce expanded delivery options. The next period will bring new destination points west of Colton, California, made available through a joint tariff with an SFPP, L.P. segment that will be reconfigured to support westbound flows between Watson and Colton, ensuring shippers can reach the Los Angeles market more directly.The Western Gateway project comprises a new-build segment from Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona and the integration of Kinder Morgan's existing SFPP, L.P. pipeline from Colton to Phoenix. As part of the plan, the SFPP, L.P. segment will be reversed to move refined products from east to west into California, aligning with the project’s objective to shift flows toward West Coast demand centers. The system will draw supply from Borger and El Paso, Texas, connecting midcontinent refining with Southwest demand centers, and will include reversing the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to route midcontinent products into Western Gateway.Once complete, the initiative is designed to create a robust transport corridor linking Midwest refining with growth markets in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas, via the CALNEV extension. The project seeks to diversify supply, improve energy security, and support sustained demand growth across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
