Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH4-BNG3-S9G3-61M2-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS: KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

+ WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE GAINS MOMENTUM, LINKING MIDWEST REFINERIES TO WESTERN MARKETS

The initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline has closed with strong market interest, and Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan say the response supports advancing the project. A second open season is planned for January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and expanded delivery options, including new destinations west of Colton through a joint tariff with SFPP, L.P. that reconfigures the Colton-Phoenix segment to support westbound flows.The Western Gateway includes a new Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona line and the reversal of the SFPP, L.P. Colton-Phoenix segment. The plan also reverses the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to feed midcontinent refineries and supply Western Gateway’s western deliveries.The project aims to connect Midwest refining capacity with growing demand in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing supply diversity and energy security across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JH3-HP93-S4TB-X1MW-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

+ OPEN SEASON SUCCESS SPURS NEXT PHASE FOR WESTERN GATEWAY REFINED-PRODUCTS CORRIDOR

Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan announced that the initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline attracted strong market interest and solid shipper commitments, reinforcing demand for added refined-products transportation capacity into the Southwest and California. The partners said the response warrants moving forward with a second open season in January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and to introduce expanded delivery options. The next period will bring new destination points west of Colton, California, made available through a joint tariff with an SFPP, L.P. segment that will be reconfigured to support westbound flows between Watson and Colton, ensuring shippers can reach the Los Angeles market more directly.The Western Gateway project comprises a new-build segment from Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona and the integration of Kinder Morgan's existing SFPP, L.P. pipeline from Colton to Phoenix. As part of the plan, the SFPP, L.P. segment will be reversed to move refined products from east to west into California, aligning with the project’s objective to shift flows toward West Coast demand centers. The system will draw supply from Borger and El Paso, Texas, connecting midcontinent refining with Southwest demand centers, and will include reversing the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to route midcontinent products into Western Gateway.Once complete, the initiative is designed to create a robust transport corridor linking Midwest refining with growth markets in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas, via the CALNEV extension. The project seeks to diversify supply, improve energy security, and support sustained demand growth across the western United States.

Alcoa Corp (NYSE:AA) drops 1.8% on slipping relative strength

Alcoa Corp's (NYSE: AA $62.61) stock price dropped $1.17 (1.8%) from its previous trading session to close at $62.61. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 34.0 points (0.5%) in the day, the relative price change was -2.3%. In the NYSE market, the stock's relative price strength percentile is slipping at 96 for 6 months, 64 for 3 months, and 59 for 1 month. A price fall combined with a slipping relative strength is a bearish signal. There were 2,519,112 shares worth $157.7 million traded today; the volume was 0.4 times the average daily trading volume of 5.8 million shares. In the past three days trading has been on consistently low volumes; Volume Index (VI) has been 0.5 or lower.

+ Alcoa Corp (AA: $62.63) Market Cap $16 billion, Basic materials sector, P/E: 12.1, PV$1000 (5 yrs): $1,860

Activities

Alcoa Corporation is a leading global producer of bauxite, alumina, and aluminum, headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company operates across the full aluminum value chain, encompassing bauxite mining, alumina refining, and aluminum smelting and casting, serving diverse industries such as aerospace, automotive, packaging, and construction. Alcoa's core activities emphasize sustainable production practices, including low-carbon aluminum manufacturing to meet environmental standards. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been its aluminum products for electric vehicles and renewable energy infrastructure, driven by rising demand for lightweight, eco-friendly materials that enhance energy efficiency. This strategic focus has positioned Alcoa to capitalize on global trends toward decarbonization and electrification, while maintaining operational excellence and innovation in its core processes. It is the NYSE's 3rd largest Basic materials company by market capitalisation.

Corteva Inc (NYSE:CTVA) decreases 0.2% in today's session

Corteva Inc's (NYSE: CTVA $80.86) stock price decreased 15.0c (0.2%) from its previous trading session to close at $80.86. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 34.0 points (0.5%) in the day, the relative price change was -0.7%. There were 2,501,120 shares worth $202.2 million traded today; the volume was 0.6 times the average daily trading volume of 4 million shares.

+ Corteva Inc (CTVA: $80.85) Market Cap $54 billion, Basic materials sector, P/E: 51.2, PV$1000 (5 yrs): $1,824

Activities

Corteva Inc., a leading global agriculture company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, specializes in developing and delivering innovative solutions to enhance crop productivity and sustainability. As a pure-play agricultural firm spun off from DowDuPont in 2019, Corteva focuses on three core business segments: seed and trait technologies, crop protection products, and digital agriculture tools. The company offers a wide range of high-performance seeds for crops like corn, soybeans, and wheat, alongside herbicides, insecticides, and fungicides designed to protect yields while minimizing environmental impact. Among its fastest-growing segments are digital farming solutions, such as precision agriculture platforms and data analytics, which leverage AI and IoT to optimize farming practices, as well as biological products that promote sustainable pest management. Corteva's commitment to research and development has positioned it as a key player in addressing global food security challenges, serving farmers in over 140 countries with a strong emphasis on innovation and environmental stewardship. It is the NYSE's largest Basic materials company by market capitalisation.

+ Dow Inc (DOW: $40.29) Market Cap $29 billion, Basic materials sector, PV$1000 (5 yrs): $1,051

Activities

Dow Inc., a global leader in materials science and a constituent of the New York Stock Exchange, focuses on the production and innovation of performance materials, chemicals, and plastics. Its core business activities span three primary segments: Packaging and Specialty Plastics, Industrial Intermediates and Infrastructure, and Performance Materials and Coatings, serving industries such as packaging, construction, electronics, and agriculture. The fastest-growing segments include Packaging and Specialty Plastics, driven by surging e-commerce demand and a shift toward sustainable, recyclable materials, as well as Industrial Intermediates, which benefit from infrastructure investments and advancements in automotive and electronics applications. Dow emphasizes research and development to enhance efficiency and sustainability, positioning the company for continued growth in emerging markets while maintaining a strong commitment to environmental stewardship and operational excellence. It is the NYSE's 2nd largest Basic materials company by market capitalisation.

Enviri Corporation (NYSE:NVRI) stock ticks down 0.2%

Enviri Corporation's (NYSE: NVRI $19.66) stock price ticked down 3.0c (0.2%) from its previous trading session to close at $19.66. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 21.1 points (0.3%) in the day, the relative price change was -0.4%. There were 943,600 shares worth $18.6 million traded today; the volume was 0.8 times the average daily trading volume of 1.2 million shares.

+ Enviri Corporation (NVRI: $19.66) Market Cap $2 billion, Basic materials sector, PV$1000 (10 yrs): $2,773

Activities

Enviri Corporation, listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), is a global leader in environmental solutions and sustainable industrial services, formed through the rebranding of Harsco Environmental in 2023. The company's core business activities encompass hazardous waste management, metal recovery, recycling, and circular economy initiatives, serving industries such as steel, energy, and manufacturing to minimize environmental impact and promote resource efficiency. By leveraging advanced technologies, Enviri processes industrial byproducts into reusable materials, reducing landfill use and supporting regulatory compliance. The fastest-growing segments include its recycling and metals recovery operations, driven by increasing demand for sustainable practices amid global climate goals and stricter environmental regulations, with notable expansion in e-waste management and innovative slag processing solutions that enhance operational efficiency and profitability. This strategic focus positions Enviri as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy. It is the NYSE's 10th largest Basic materials company by market capitalisation.

Materion Corporation (NYSE:MTRN) up 9.1% in 2 days

Materion Corporation's (NYSE: MTRN $188.22) stock price extended its gains on Friday, rising an additional $4.41 (2.4%) to close at $188.22. The shares have jumped $15.68 (9.1%) over the past two trading days; compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 94.2 points (1.3%) in the two days for a relative price increase of 7.8%. The 2-day rise was fuelled by a cumulative volume of 2.6 times the average 2-day volume of 185,236 shares. There were 309,300 shares worth $58.2 million traded today. Today its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 5.4% was 1.5 times its average daily volatility of 3.6%. The high for the day was $191.65 and low for the day was $181.82. In the past five days trading has been on consistently high volumes; Volume Index (VI) has been 1.2 or higher.

+ Materion Corporation (MTRN: $188.22) Market Cap $4 billion, Basic materials sector, P/E: 52.1, PV$1000 (20 yrs): $8,930

Activities

Materion Corporation is a leading U.S.-based materials science company, specializing in the production and distribution of advanced engineered materials, including beryllium, specialty metals, and alloys. Its core business activities encompass the development and supply of high-performance materials for critical applications in electronics, aerospace, defense, automotive, and energy sectors, leveraging expertise in precision optics, thin film coatings, and metal matrix composites. The company's fastest-growing segments include electronic materials and advanced coatings, fueled by increasing demand for 5G infrastructure, electric vehicles, and renewable energy technologies. This strategic focus on innovation and sustainability positions Materion to capitalize on emerging markets, enhancing its competitive edge through efficient manufacturing and strong customer partnerships. Overall, Materion's commitment to quality and technological advancement supports its role as a key supplier in high-tech industries. It is the NYSE's 8th largest Basic materials company by market capitalisation.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGC-DM23-S9SW-31BV-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND ITS PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM KINDER MORGAN, INC. AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ CFS UNIFIES HR, ERP AND EPM WITH ORACLE AI SUITE

Colonial First State (CFS), a leading Australian wealth manager, is deploying Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to modernize its operations and enhance the employee experience. As of the 24th of March 2026, CFS manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly one million Australians.The transformation consolidates systems into a unified, agile platform, expected to accelerate innovation and improve operational consistency as CFS seeks to meet client expectations.Jeroen Buwalda, Chief Operating Officer, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The implementation is seen as a catalyst for smarter workflows, better decision-making and enabling CFS to pursue strategic priorities.The integration of Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) has unified HR operations, streamlining onboarding and workforce data insights, empowering HR leaders with a single, reliable view of data to support development initiatives.

+ SHAREHOLDERS BACK 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP'S MERGER WITH MID PENN BANCORP, EXPANDING REGIONAL FOOTPRINT

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has won shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026. With more than 80% of outstanding shares represented, the vote passed decisively, with over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Chair, President and CEO Rory G. Ritrievi said the level of support for this transaction was tremendous, underscoring strong industry and investor backing for the combination.1st Colonial's President and CEO Robert White noted the merger will enhance the company's ability to serve customers and communities, while positioning Mid Penn to extend its footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and deepen its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey.Upon closing, Mid Penn is projected to have about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026. Mid Penn Bancorp, headquartered in Harrisburg, operates 59 retail locations and offers a broad suite of financial products and services, reinforcing its role in the regional banking landscape.The transaction aligns with a broader trend of consolidation in the regional banking sector, as institutions seek scale to improve market presence and operational efficiency while integration proceeds.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66 (NYSE:PSX) AND KINDER MORGAN, INC. (NYSE:KMI) MOVE FORWARD ON WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER COMMITMENTS

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The collaboration reflects a shared objective to strengthen West Coast fuel resilience amid evolving regulatory and market conditions.The Western Gateway Pipeline would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets, addressing growing demand and reducing exposure to supply disruptions. The project would also connect to Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing interregional flow of refined products.A new Borger, Texas to Phoenix line is planned, and the Colton, California to Phoenix leg would be reversed to enable east-to-west fuel movement. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. The parties have not disclosed total project costs, and service is expected to commence in mid-2029.

+ KINDER MORGAN SEES CASH-FLOW STRENGTH BACKED BY BACKLOG, AMID INSIDER-STOCK ACTIVITY

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan is balancing a robust project backlog with ongoing insider stock sales, creating a nuanced narrative for investors. The backlog, pegged at about US$10 billion and focused mainly on natural gas, positions the company to benefit from rising demand from AI-driven data centers and LNG exports.The company says the pipeline’s structure yields contracted, infrastructure-like cash flows that underpin a stable base. In its Q4 2025 results, Kinder Morgan posted record net income and adjusted EBITDA, with about 96% of cash flows coming from fee-based or hedged contracts.Nonetheless, insider activity remains a point of concern. Filings show that a senior terminal executive has continued to sell shares over the past year, prompting questions about insider confidence in growth prospects and the sustainability of a capital-intensive model amid a sizable net debt.Looking ahead, Kinder Morgan projects US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion of earnings by 2029, implying a roughly 3.9% annual growth pace, if conditions stay supportive. Analysts offer a wide range of fair-value estimates, from US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, underscoring divergent views on how the backlog could translate into value.In sum, the company’s combination of a large backlog and fee-based cash flows presents a compelling case for some investors, while insider sales and leverage inject caution for others as market conditions evolve.

+ OPEN SEASON SUCCESS SPURS NEXT PHASE FOR WESTERN GATEWAY REFINED-PRODUCTS CORRIDOR

Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan announced that the initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline attracted strong market interest and solid shipper commitments, reinforcing demand for added refined-products transportation capacity into the Southwest and California. The partners said the response warrants moving forward with a second open season in January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and to introduce expanded delivery options. The next period will bring new destination points west of Colton, California, made available through a joint tariff with an SFPP, L.P. segment that will be reconfigured to support westbound flows between Watson and Colton, ensuring shippers can reach the Los Angeles market more directly.The Western Gateway project comprises a new-build segment from Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona and the integration of Kinder Morgan's existing SFPP, L.P. pipeline from Colton to Phoenix. As part of the plan, the SFPP, L.P. segment will be reversed to move refined products from east to west into California, aligning with the project’s objective to shift flows toward West Coast demand centers. The system will draw supply from Borger and El Paso, Texas, connecting midcontinent refining with Southwest demand centers, and will include reversing the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to route midcontinent products into Western Gateway.Once complete, the initiative is designed to create a robust transport corridor linking Midwest refining with growth markets in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas, via the CALNEV extension. The project seeks to diversify supply, improve energy security, and support sustained demand growth across the western United States.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JGG-8H13-RRXY-F1M0-00000-00COLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisSTOCKCOLONIAL GROUP INC. - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: COLONIAL GROUP

PermID4296910829Websitehttp://www.colonialgroupinc.comIndustryDistributor Of Petroleum ProductsAddress101 N Lathrop Ave, Savannah, GA 31415

ACTIVITIES:

Colonial Group Inc., founded in 1921, is a family-owned energy and logistics company headquartered in Savannah, Georgia. Employing over 1,000 people, it operates across the Southeast, providing fuel distribution, storage, and marine services. Its subsidiaries, including Colonial Oil and Enmarket, serve energy, retail, and transportation sectors. With a focus on reliability, Colonial manages terminals and pipelines, distributing petroleum products to commercial and retail clients. In 2024, it expanded its renewable fuel offerings, aligning with sustainability trends. Its culture emphasizes safety, community engagement, and employee development, with charitable initiatives supporting local causes. Competing with Kinder Morgan, Colonial stands out for its regional expertise and integrated services. Recent investments in green energy and logistics technology enhance its market position.

+ COLONIAL GROUP [2 News That Matters]

+ AUSTRALIAN WEALTH MANAGER PARTNERS WITH ORACLE AI TO BOOST EFFICIENCY

Colonial First State (CFS) is investing in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications to transform its operations. The Australian wealth manager manages AU $181 billion in assets for nearly a million Australians as of the 24th of March 2026.The move consolidates HR, ERP and EPM into a single, unified platform, driving streamlined processes and data-driven decision-making.Jeroen Buwalda, COO, said, "By modernizing our core systems, we are giving our people more time to focus on supporting our members." The rollout is expected to accelerate workflows and support strategic initiatives.Meredith Rowan, Group Vice President at Oracle ANZ and ASEAN, noted that CFS is enhancing its operational agility and data-driven insights, essential for navigating the evolving wealth-management landscape. With these advancements, Colonial First State is poised to meet rising client expectations and maintain a competitive edge.

+ STRATEGIC BANKING MERGER APPROVED: 1ST COLONIAL BANCORP AND MID PENN BANCORP

1st Colonial Bancorp, Inc. has received shareholder approval for its merger with Mid Penn Bancorp, Inc. at a special meeting held on 11th February 2026, with more than 80% of outstanding shares represented and over 99% voting in favor, according to the inspector of elections.Mid Penn's Rory G. Ritrievi commented that the support for the transaction was tremendous, and 1st Colonial's Robert White said the merger will strengthen the bank's ability to serve customers and communities.The merger is designed to extend Mid Penn's footprint into the Greater Philadelphia area and bolster its presence in Southeastern Pennsylvania and Southern New Jersey. After closing, Mid Penn is expected to reach about $7.5 billion in assets as of 31 March 2026, with 59 retail locations in Harrisburg-based operations.The deal reflects ongoing consolidation in the regional banking sector as both banks prepare for the integration.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KINDER MORGAN [3 News That Matters]

+ PHILLIPS 66, KINDER MORGAN BUILD WESTERN GATEWAY FUEL PIPELINE BACKED BY SHIPPER PLEDGES

Apr. 20, 2026 — Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) and Kinder Morgan, Inc. (NYSE:KMI) Reports progress on the Western Gateway Pipeline after securing long-term shipper commitments to move forward with the project to supply fuel to Arizona and California. The project targets a new cross-regional fuel corridor to alleviate reliance on limited California pipeline links.The plan would connect Midwest and Gulf Coast refinery supply directly to Phoenix and California markets and will tie into Las Vegas through CALNEV. It contemplates constructing a Borger, Texas to Phoenix line and reversing the Colton, California to Phoenix segment to enable east-to-west transport into California. Phillips 66 would reverse its Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to support the new system. Total cost remains undisclosed and service is slated for mid-2029.

+ BACKLOG-DRIVEN GROWTH AT KINDER MORGAN INTERSECTS WITH INSIDER SALES CONCERNS

As of 9th April 2026, Kinder Morgan’s US$10 billion backlog, centered on natural gas, is positioned to benefit from AI-enabled data centers and LNG exports.The company reported Q4 2025 results with record net income and adjusted EBITDA, noting that about 96% of cash flows come from fee-based or hedged contracts.Insider activity has continued, with a senior terminal executive selling shares over the past year, while the company targets US$19 billion in revenue and US$3.5 billion in earnings by 2029, a roughly 3.9% annual growth trajectory subject to market conditions and asset-management execution.Fair-value estimates among analysts range from about US$34.82 to US$48.57 per share, reflecting divergent views on the backlog's ability to translate into value.

+ WESTERN GATEWAY PIPELINE GAINS MOMENTUM, LINKING MIDWEST REFINERIES TO WESTERN MARKETS

The initial open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline has closed with strong market interest, and Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan say the response supports advancing the project. A second open season is planned for January 2026 to offer remaining capacity and expanded delivery options, including new destinations west of Colton through a joint tariff with SFPP, L.P. that reconfigures the Colton-Phoenix segment to support westbound flows.The Western Gateway includes a new Borger, Texas to Phoenix, Arizona line and the reversal of the SFPP, L.P. Colton-Phoenix segment. The plan also reverses the Gold Pipeline from Borger to St. Louis to feed midcontinent refineries and supply Western Gateway’s western deliveries.The project aims to connect Midwest refining capacity with growing demand in Phoenix, California, and Las Vegas via CALNEV, enhancing supply diversity and energy security across the western United States.

value.Title: Citing 'unsustainable stress' from price volatility, grid operator PJM lays out reform options

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHS-SYY3-SD4J-133N-00000-00Citing 'unsustainable stress' from price volatility, grid operator PJM lays out reform options2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:08:55ZLexisNexisCiting 'unsustainable stress' from price volatility, grid operator PJM lays out reform options

Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Peter Hall

May 8th, 2026 ( Ohio Capital Journal - Delivered by Newstex )

Constellation Energy's Eddystone Generating Station in Delaware County is one of two fossil fuel fired power plants ordered by U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright to remain ready to produce electricity beyond their retirement dates. (Photo by Peter Hall/Capital-Star)

The nation's largest electricity grid operator has called on power plant operators, investors, utilities and consumers to consider reforms to ensure the region's wholesale electricity market can supply enough power as it faces unprecedented demand from data centers.

PJM Interconnection, based in the Philadelphia suburbs near Valley Forge, has been under scrutiny as record electricity prices in its auctions contributed to skyrocketing bills for consumers and businesses in the last year.

In a report issued Wednesday, PJM said the growing demand from data centers and broader electrification of the economy is exacerbated by tightening supply as older, dirtier power plants retire and supply chain and permitting issues make new plants harder to build fast enough.

'The PJM region is now navigating a convergence of three structural forces that have pushed the system into disequilibrium,' the report says. 'The result is a transition from an era of managing surplus to an era of managing scarcity - one that is anticipated to persist for some time based on current projections.'

PJM's electricity pricing model depends on a concept it calls the 'shared reliability compact' in which all customers, large and small, share the same standard of reliability and agree to pay to ensure it.

The organization's board recognized price volatility - while economically rational - is creating unsustainable stress on the compact and called on PJM's staff to reexamine its foundational assumptions in a resource-constrained world.

It also suggested that government intervention in the market has kept investors on the sidelines by undermining the credibility of economic signals that would normally spur the construction of new generation plants.

In 2024, Gov. Josh Shapiro sued PJM demanding a limit on prices after an auction in July of that year resulted in a record price. Federal regulators agreed last week to extend the price control for a second time through 2030.

Shapiro pushed back on the notion that his administration's intervention has dissuaded new investment.

'A core reason why the reforms described in this report are needed is that PJM hasn't been moving fast enough to connect new resources for many years and continues to deny states a full seat at the table,' spokeswoman Rosie Lapowsky said in a statement.

Transmission lines along a highway in Lancaster County on October 14, 2025. (Photo by Jessica Kourkounis for the Pennsylvania Capital-Star)

'The Shapiro administration looks forward to working constructively with fellow states and with PJM on next steps, but Gov. Shapiro is never going to let Pennsylvanians get stuck with a bill they don't deserve,' the statement said. 'That's why any changes must prioritize reliability, protect consumers and increase transparency.'

Jon Gordon, director of the renewable energy industry group Advanced Energy United, said PJM finds itself in a tough spot and is taking fire from many elected leaders. He said the report is a self-reflective document.

'They're trying to lay out the facts of the world. They want everyone involved in the solution.

These are really big, profound decisions,' Gordon said. 'Time is short and we need to solve these problems yesterday, and that's where the challenge will be.'

PJM, which manages the grid for 13 states, including Pennsylvania, and Washington, D.C., stopped short of offering a solution, but invited stakeholders to join in discussion to restore confidence in the system.

'Wholesale electricity markets are extraordinary institutions, and their most essential infrastructure is not a price curve or a performance obligation - it is legitimacy,' PJM President and CEO David Mills said in a letter accompanying the report.

'Generators, utilities, investors and consumers must all believe, at a basic level, that the rules are fair, stable and the product of a process they recognize as credible.' Mills continued. 'It is built through the kind of deliberation this paper is intended to initiate - and it is the only foundation on which a durable market design can rest.'

The paper identifies three possible pathways for reform:

The first would stabilize the market by ensuring that the vast majority of power needed is obtained through longer-term agreements to insulate ratepayers from volatility. Any additional power needed would be secured through spot auctions to allow higher prices to ensure capacity is available for peaks in demand on the hottest and coldest days of the year.

Another would do away with the shared reliability contract when supplies are scarce and develop a framework that differentiates between customers that can and cannot be cut off. Policymakers, including the PJM governors and the Trump administration, have backed a requirement for large loads such as data centers to build their own power plants.

'Path B focuses on physical accountability - those who do not bring or fund supply cannot lean indefinitely on the shared pool - but it requires a fundamental reorientation of how the PJM system allocates reliability as a scarce good,' the report says.

The third would pair long-term contracts that reduce volatility with a shift in how power generation owners recover revenue to cover the cost of producing electricity, placing an emphasis on payments for the underlying electricity commodity rather than the availability of power plants to handle peaks in demand.

This story was originally produced by Pennsylvania Capital-Star, which is part of States Newsroom, a nonprofit news network which includes Ohio Capital Journal, and is supported by grants and a coalition of donors as a 501c(3) public charity.

Ryan Deto

May 14th, 2026 ( Axios - Delivered by Newstex )

Sen. Dave McCormick, a Republican, introduced a bill late last month to cut costs and speed up production of energy projects across the nation.

Why it matters: Electricity and gas utility prices are rising, and some blame the tepid buildout of power plants as the cause.

The big picture: The Unlock American Energy and Jobs Act seeks to release $1 trillion in infrastructure projects currently tied up in federal permitting, according to McCormick.

He said if the bill becomes law, it could create $2.4 trillion in economic activity and thousands of new jobs.

What he's saying: "It takes five to six years to get a permit. This is ridiculous," said McCormick at a roundtable with local labor leaders in Downtown Pittsburgh last week.

Zoom in: The bill would set a one-year deadline on state review of Clean Water Act requirements for energy projects. It would also:

Speed up approvals and limit veto points for liquid natural gas export projects.

Limit National Environmental Policy Act lawsuits, set deadlines for resolving such lawsuits, and prevent courts from vacating approvals while legal challenges are pending.

Extend licensing agreements for nuclear plants from 40 to 60 years since many of them have been operating longer than 40 years already, like the Beaver Valley Power Station.

Between the lines: McCormick has helped broker billions of dollars in energy investment for Pennsylvania. He said energy companies want to invest, and cost isn't the main issue for them; it's how long it takes to get projects approved and completed.

The other side: The bill has a strong focus on speeding up natural gas projects. Environmental groups argue that only stimulating natural gas production would take the focus away from building renewable energy power plants, which advocates say can be built faster and cheaper than fossil fuel and nuclear plants.

The bottom line: The Unlock American Energy and Jobs Act still needs approval in Congress and President Trump's signature to become law.

urn:contentItem:6JHV-JGT3-S2F2-P3NH-00000-00NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) Advances Gas and Nuclear Strategy With Japan-Backed US Projects2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisNextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) Advances Gas and Nuclear Strategy With Japan-Backed US Projects

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

07 May 2026 14:16 EDT

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is pursuing two Japan-backed natural gas-fired power projects in the United States, with agreements expected to be finalized within about three months, according to reports on May 5, 2026. The projects are to be located in Pennsylvania and Texas and are designed to support growing demand from data centers, with a combined capacity of nearly 10 gigawatts

NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE) is expanding its portfolio beyond traditional generation as Florida Power & Light, its regulated utility, experiences rising data-center demand. FPL's pipeline now stands at 21 gigawatts, and the company is in advanced discussions to fulfill more than half of that demand, with projects expected to be operational by 2028. The company also aims to revive the Duane Arnold nuclear plant in Iowa to power Google data centers, while NextEra Energy Resources has added 4 gigawatts of renewable and storage projects, lifting the backlog to about 33 gigawatts. In the first quarter, NextEra Energy posted adjusted earnings per share of $1.09, above analysts' expectations of $0.96

SECTION 2 NEXTERA ENERGY ACTIVITIES

Activities

Nextera Energy, Inc., a Fortune 200 company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, is a leading clean energy provider in the United States, specializing in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity. As one of the world's largest producers of renewable energy, its core operations encompass a diverse portfolio that includes wind, solar, nuclear, and natural gas power plants, serving millions of customers across Florida and other regions. The company's fastest-growing segments are its renewable energy initiatives, particularly utility-scale solar and wind projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory environments. This growth is driven by strategic investments in innovative technologies and infrastructure, positioning Nextera as a pioneer in the energy transition. With a strong commitment to operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, Nextera continues to deliver reliable energy solutions while advancing its goal of reducing carbon emissions. It is the NYSE's largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JHH-GV63-S3X1-G421-00000-00Citing 'unsustainable stress' from price volatility, grid operator PJM lays out reform options2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisCiting 'unsustainable stress' from price volatility, grid operator PJM lays out reform options

The views expressed in any and all content distributed by Newstex and its re-distributors (collectively, the "Newstex Authoritative Content") are solely those of the respective author(s) and not necessarily the views of Newstex or its re-distributors. Stories from such authors are provided "AS IS," with no warranties, and confer no rights. The material and information provided in Newstex Authoritative Content are for general information only and should not, in any respect, be relied on as professional advice. Newstex Authoritative Content is not "read and approved" before it is posted. Accordingly, neither Newstex nor its re-distributors make any claims, promises or guarantees about the accuracy, completeness, or adequacy of the information contained therein or linked to from such content, nor do they take responsibility for any aspect of such content. The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Peter Hall

May 6th, 2026 ( Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Delivered by Newstex )

Constellation Energy's Eddystone Generating Station in Delaware County is one of two fossil fuel fired power plants ordered by U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright to remain ready to produce electricity beyond their retirement dates. (Photo by Peter Hall/Capital-Star)

The nation's largest electricity grid operator has called on power plant operators, investors, utilities and consumers to consider reforms to ensure the region's wholesale electricity market can supply enough power as it faces unprecedented demand from data centers.

PJM Interconnection, based in the Philadelphia suburbs near Valley Forge, has been under scrutiny as record electricity prices in its auctions contributed to skyrocketing bills for consumers and businesses in the last year.

In a report issued Wednesday, PJM said the growing demand from data centers and broader electrification of the economy is exacerbated by tightening supply as older, dirtier power plants retire and supply chain and permitting issues make new plants harder to build fast enough.

'The PJM region is now navigating a convergence of three structural forces that have pushed the system into disequilibrium,' the report says. 'The result is a transition from an era of managing surplus to an era of managing scarcity - one that is anticipated to persist for some time based on current projections.'

Support independent reporting on Pennsylvania state government that impacts communities statewide.

SUPPORT

PJM's electricity pricing model depends on a concept it calls the 'shared reliability compact' in which all customers, large and small, share the same standard of reliability and agree to pay to ensure it.

The organization's board recognized price volatility - while economically rational - is creating unsustainable stress on the compact and called on PJM's staff to reexamine its foundational assumptions in a resource-constrained world.

It also suggested that government intervention in the market has kept investors on the sidelines by undermining the credibility of economic signals that would normally spur the construction of new generation plants.

In 2024, Gov. Josh Shapiro sued PJM demanding a limit on prices after an auction in July of that year resulted in a record price. Federal regulators agreed last week to extend the price control for a second time through 2030.

Shapiro pushed back on the notion that his administration's intervention has dissuaded new investment.

'A core reason why the reforms described in this report are needed is that PJM hasn't been moving fast enough to connect new resources for many years and continues to deny states a full seat at the table,' spokeswoman Rosie Lapowsky said in a statement.

Transmission lines along a highway in Lancaster County on October 14, 2025. (Photo by Jessica Kourkounis for the Pennsylvania Capital-Star)

'The Shapiro administration looks forward to working constructively with fellow states and with PJM on next steps, but Gov. Shapiro is never going to let Pennsylvanians get stuck with a bill they don't deserve,' the statement said. 'That's why any changes must prioritize reliability, protect consumers and increase transparency.'

Jon Gordon, director of the renewable energy industry group Advanced Energy United, said PJM finds itself in a tough spot and is taking fire from many elected leaders. He said the report is a self-reflective document.

'They're trying to lay out the facts of the world. They want everyone involved in the solution.

These are really big, profound decisions,' Gordon said. 'Time is short and we need to solve these problems yesterday, and that's where the challenge will be.'

PJM, which manages the grid for 13 states, including Pennsylvania, and Washington, D.C., stopped short of offering a solution, but invited stakeholders to join in discussion to restore confidence in the system.

'Wholesale electricity markets are extraordinary institutions, and their most essential infrastructure is not a price curve or a performance obligation - it is legitimacy,' PJM President and CEO David Mills said in a letter accompanying the report.

'Generators, utilities, investors and consumers must all believe, at a basic level, that the rules are fair, stable and the product of a process they recognize as credible.' Mills continued. 'It is built through the kind of deliberation this paper is intended to initiate - and it is the only foundation on which a durable market design can rest.'

Don't count on social media algorithms to help you find a trusted news source. Sign up for our free morning newsletter.

SUBSCRIBE

The paper identifies three possible pathways for reform:

The first would stabilize the market by ensuring that the vast majority of power needed is obtained through longer-term agreements to insulate ratepayers from volatility. Any additional power needed would be secured through spot auctions to allow higher prices to ensure capacity is available for peaks in demand on the hottest and coldest days of the year.

Another would do away with the shared reliability contract when supplies are scarce and develop a framework that differentiates between customers that can and cannot be cut off. Policymakers, including the PJM governors and the Trump administration, have backed a requirement for large loads such as data centers to build their own power plants.

'Path B focuses on physical accountability - those who do not bring or fund supply cannot lean indefinitely on the shared pool - but it requires a fundamental reorientation of how the PJM system allocates reliability as a scarce good,' the report says.

The third would pair long-term contracts that reduce volatility with a shift in how power generation owners recover revenue to cover the cost of producing electricity, placing an emphasis on payments for the underlying electricity commodity rather than the availability of power plants to handle peaks in demand.

Link to the original story.

Environmental Power OTCMKTS:EPGRQ - Get Free Report and Montauk Renewables NASDAQ:MNTK - Get Free Report are both energy companies, but which is the better investment? We will contrast the two businesses based on the strength of their valuation, profitability, risk, analyst recommendations, institutional ownership, dividends and earnings.

Analyst Recommendations

This is a breakdown of recent ratings and recommmendations for Environmental Power and Montauk Renewables, as provided by MarketBeat. Sell RatingsHold RatingsBuy RatingsStrong Buy RatingsRating ScoreEnvironmental Power00000.00Montauk Renewables13012.20

Montauk Renewables has a consensus target price of $1.80, indicating a potential upside of 28.57%. Given Montauk Renewables' stronger consensus rating and higher probable upside, analysts plainly believe Montauk Renewables is more favorable than Environmental Power.

Earnings and Valuation

This table compares Environmental Power and Montauk Renewables"s gross revenue, earnings per share and valuation. Gross RevenuePrice/Sales RatioNet IncomeEarnings Per SharePrice/Earnings RatioEnvironmental PowerN/AN/AN/AN/AN/AMontauk Renewables$176.38 million1.14$1.75 million$0.0270.00

Profitability

Institutional and Insider Ownership

16.4% of Montauk Renewables shares are owned by institutional investors. 18.8% of Environmental Power shares are owned by insiders. Comparatively, 54.3% of Montauk Renewables shares are owned by insiders. Strong institutional ownership is an indication that endowments, large money managers and hedge funds believe a company is poised for long-term growth.

Summary

Montauk Renewables beats Environmental Power on 9 of the 9 factors compared between the two stocks.

About Environmental Power Get Free Report

Environmental Power Corporation engages in the development, ownership, and operation of renewable energy production facilities in the United States. The company develops renewable energy facilities for the production and commercial application of methane-rich biogas produced from animal, food industry, and other organic wastes. The biogas could be sold to end-users or used to produce pipeline-grade methane, which is referred as renewable natural gas, liquefied natural gas, compressed natural gas, and renewable electrical energy or thermal energy, as well as to produce other useful by-products. Environmental Power Corporation operates three single digester facilities in Wisconsin. The company was founded in 1982 and is headquartered in Tarrytown, New York.

About Montauk Renewables Get Free Report

Montauk Renewables, Inc., a renewable energy company, engages in recovery and processing of biogas from landfills and other non-fossil fuel sources. It operates in two segments, Renewable Natural Gas and Renewable Electricity Generation. The company develops, owns, and operates renewable natural gas RNG projects that captures methane and prevents it from being released into the atmosphere by converting it into either RNG or electrical power for the electrical grid. Its customers for RNG and renewable identification numbers RIN include large, long-term owner-operators of landfills and livestock farms, local utilities, and large refiners in the natural gas and refining sectors. Montauk Renewables, Inc. was founded in 1980 and is headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.

Fold ( NASDAQ:FLD - Get Free Report) and AlTi Global ( NASDAQ:ALTI - Get Free Report) are both small-cap finance companies, but which is the superior business? We will contrast the two businesses based on the strength of their institutional ownership, profitability, earnings, risk, dividends, valuation and analyst recommendations.

Earnings and Valuation

Analyst Ratings

This is a breakdown of current recommendations and price targets for Fold and AlTi Global, as provided by MarketBeat.

Fold currently has a consensus price target of $5.00, indicating a potential upside of 264.96%. Given Fold's stronger consensus rating and higher possible upside, analysts clearly believe Fold is more favorable than AlTi Global.

Institutional and Insider Ownership

56.9% of Fold shares are held by institutional investors. Comparatively, 3.6% of AlTi Global shares are held by institutional investors. 23.1% of Fold shares are held by company insiders. Comparatively, 10.5% of AlTi Global shares are held by company insiders. Strong institutional ownership is an indication that endowments, hedge funds and large money managers believe a company will outperform the market over the long term.

Volatility and Risk

Fold has a beta of 0.39, suggesting that its stock price is 61% less volatile than the S&P 500. Comparatively, AlTi Global has a beta of 0.76, suggesting that its stock price is 24% less volatile than the S&P 500.

Summary

Fold beats AlTi Global on 8 of the 15 factors compared between the two stocks.

About Fold

( Get Free Report)

FTAC Emerald Acquisition Corp. does not have significant operations. It intends to effect a merger, capital stock exchange, asset acquisition, stock purchase, reorganization, and similar business combination with one or more businesses or assets operating in the clean/renewable energy, water sustainability, agricultural technology, shared economy software, and mobility sectors. The company was formerly known as Emerald ESG Acquisition Corp. FTAC Emerald Acquisition Corp. was incorporated in 2021 and is based in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

About AlTi Global

( Get Free Report)

AlTi Global, Inc. provides wealth and asset management services individuals, families, foundations, and institutions in the United States, the United Kingdom, and internationally. It operates through two segments, Wealth Management and Strategic Alternatives. The company offers discretionary investment management, non-discretionary investment advisory, and investment management and advisory services. It also provides trust and administration services, such as entity formation and management; creating or modifying trust instruments and administrative practices to meet beneficiary needs; corporate, trustee-executor, and fiduciary services; provision of directors and company secretarial services; administering entity ownership of intellectual property rights; advisory and administration services in connection with investments in marine and aviation assets; and administering entity ownership of fine art and collectibles. In addition, the company offers family office services comprising bookkeeping and back office services, private foundation management and grant making, oversight of trust administration, financial tracking and reporting, cash flow management and bill pay, and other financial services, as well as clients estate and wealth planning, family governance and education, and philanthropic and strategic services. It also provides ancillary fund management services, including investments, financial planning and strategy, sales and marketing, and back and middle office infrastructure and administration. The company offers strategic advisory, corporate advisory, brokerage, and placement agency services; structures, arranges, and provides investors with co-investment opportunities in various alternative assets; manages and advises public and private investment funds, as well as alternatives platform. The company was formerly known as Alvarium Tiedemann Holdings, Inc. and changed its name to AlTi Global, Inc. in April 2023. The company is based in New York, New York.

urn:contentItem:6JHM-3HK3-RX4C-P4GR-00000-00Alcoa Corp (NYSE:AA) Posts $1.25 Billion Credit Facility Extension Through 20282026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:10:31ZLexisNexisAlcoa Corp (NYSE:AA) Posts $1.25 Billion Credit Facility Extension Through 2028

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 10:17 EDT

Alcoa Corp (NYSE:AA) Posts a $1.25 Billion Revolving Credit Facility Extension Through 2028, disclosed on 4th May 2026. The extension keeps the $1.25 billion facility in place through 2028, reinforcing the company's liquidity in a competitive aluminum market and supporting ongoing operations and strategic initiatives.

Alcoa notes that the extension aligns with its financial strategy to maintain a robust balance sheet while investing in sustainable practices. The company's recent super composite rating, which weighs fundamentals and valuation, underscores its market position and signals resilience in the face of price fluctuations.

Investors welcomed the move, with Alcoa's shares rising on the news as the company exercises greater financial flexibility to fund growth opportunities and manage cyclicality in aluminum demand

SECTION 1 ALCOA RECENT PRESS RELEASES

SECTION 2 ALCOA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 ALCOA RECENT PRESS RELEASES

March 02: Alcoa announces dividend

Alcoa today announced a quarterly dividend of 10.0c per share. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, March 10, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, March 10, 2026 and it is payable on Thursday, March 26.

March 02: Alcoa announces dividend

Alcoa today announced a quarterly dividend of 10.0c per share. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, March 10, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, March 10, 2026 and it is payable on Thursday, March 26.

October 24, 2025: Alcoa quarterly dividends steady

Alcoa today announced a quarterly dividend of 10.0c per share for Q3/2025; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q2/2025) dividend of 10.0c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, November 04, 2025 and the record date is Tuesday, November 04, 2025 and it is payable on Friday, November 21.

SECTION 2 ALCOA ACTIVITIES

Activities

Alcoa Corporation is a leading global producer of bauxite, alumina, and aluminum, headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange. The company operates across the full aluminum value chain, encompassing bauxite mining, alumina refining, and aluminum smelting and casting, serving diverse industries such as aerospace, automotive, packaging, and construction. Alcoa's core activities emphasize sustainable production practices, including low-carbon aluminum manufacturing to meet environmental standards. In recent years, the fastest-growing segments have been its aluminum products for electric vehicles and renewable energy infrastructure, driven by rising demand for lightweight, eco-friendly materials that enhance energy efficiency. This strategic focus has positioned Alcoa to capitalize on global trends toward decarbonization and electrification, while maintaining operational excellence and innovation in its core processes. It is the NYSE's 3rd largest Basic materials company by market capitalisation.
